What belongs in the Biotonne and what does not?

activities.title: Die Mülltrennung
activities.intro: German households separate waste into several bins. Food waste, paper and packaging each have their own container, glass is taken to a public container and sorted by colour, and many bottles and cans carry a deposit that you get back in the shop.

items.hr: In die Biotonne kommen Essensreste und die Schalen von Obst und Gemüse.
items.en: Food leftovers and the peel of fruit and vegetables go into the organic bin.
items.note: In etwas kommen is the normal way to say what belongs in a bin.

items.hr: Papier und Kartons kommen in die blaue Tonne, aber schmutziges Papier gehört in den Restmüll.
items.en: Paper and cardboard go into the blue bin, but dirty paper belongs in the general waste.
items.note: der Restmüll is everything that fits in no other bin.

items.hr: Verpackungen aus Plastik und Metall kommen in den gelben Sack oder in die gelbe Tonne.
items.en: Packaging made of plastic and metal goes into the yellow bag or the yellow bin.
items.note: Some places use der gelbe Sack, others die gelbe Tonne. Both take the same things.

items.hr: Altglas bringt man zum Container und sortiert es nach Farben: weiß, grün und braun.
items.en: Waste glass is taken to the container and sorted by colour: white, green and brown.
items.note: das Altglas. Blue glass goes into the green container.

items.hr: Auf viele Flaschen und Dosen zahlt man Pfand und bekommt das Geld im Geschäft zurück.
items.en: On many bottles and cans you pay a deposit and get the money back in the shop.
items.note: das Pfand is usually 8, 15 or 25 Cent per bottle.

activities.title: Welche Tonne?
activities.intro: Practise sorting waste and the accusative after in when something goes somewhere.

questions.prompt: Wohin kommt eine leere Plastikverpackung?
questions.explanation: Packaging made of plastic or metal belongs in the yellow bag or yellow bin, not in the general waste.

questions.prompt: Was macht man mit einer Flasche mit Pfand?
options: Man bringt sie ins Geschäft zurück und bekommt Geld.
Man wirft sie in den Restmüll.
Man stellt sie in die Biotonne.
Man gibt sie beim Vermieter ab.
questions.answer: Man bringt sie ins Geschäft zurück und bekommt Geld.
questions.explanation: Pfand is a deposit you already paid at the till. The machine in the shop pays it back when you return the bottle.

questions.prompt: Essensreste und die Schalen von Obst kommen in die ___.
questions.explanation: die Biotonne takes organic waste. Plastic bags do not belong in it, even so called biodegradable ones.

questions.prompt: Grüne, braune und weiße Flaschen sortiert man nach Farben und bringt sie zum ___.
questions.explanation: Glass is not collected at the door in most places. You carry it to a public container and sort it by colour.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Paper and cardboard boxes go into the blue bin.'
questions.explanation: in plus movement takes the accusative, so die blaue Tonne, and the adjective after die keeps the ending -e.

activities.title: Der Müll im Hof
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour finds you standing in front of four different bins and explains the system; ask about packaging, glass and the deposit.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vorsicht, die alte Zeitung darf nicht in den Restmüll.
lines.en: Careful, the old newspaper must not go into the general waste.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oh, entschuldigen Sie. Und wohin kommen die leeren Joghurtbecher?
lines.en: Oh, excuse me. And where do the empty yoghurt pots go?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Verpackungen aus Plastik kommen in die gelbe Tonne dort hinten.
lines.en: Plastic packaging goes into the yellow bin over there.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Verstanden. Und die Schalen von Obst und Gemüse in die Biotonne, oder?
lines.en: Understood. And fruit and vegetable peel goes into the organic bin, right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Genau. Aber bitte ohne Plastiktüte, nur in Zeitungspapier.
lines.en: Exactly. But please without a plastic bag, only in newspaper.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut zu wissen. Ich habe auch noch viele leere Flaschen.
lines.en: Good to know. I also still have a lot of empty bottles.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bei Pfandflaschen bekommen Sie im Geschäft Geld zurück. Der Rest kommt zum Altglascontainer.
lines.en: For deposit bottles you get money back in the shop. The rest goes to the glass container.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Und dort sortiere ich nach Farben: weiß, grün und braun. Vielen Dank für die Hilfe!
lines.en: And there I sort by colour: white, green and brown. Thank you very much for your help!

days.title: Eine Reparatur melden
days.objective: Report a defect to the landlord or the property management in correct written German.
days.paretoFocus: A repair only counts once it is reported in writing, and the four fixed phrases of a formal German message are the same for every problem you will ever have.
drills: Write a short formal message about a broken heater, with opening, problem, request and closing.
List five things in a flat that can break, each with its article.
Practise saying since when the problem has existed, using seit plus dative.

items: How do you open a formal message when you do not know the name?
How do you close it?
Which case follows seit?

activities.title: Einen Schaden melden
activities.intro: A written defect report follows a fixed shape: formal greeting, who you are, what is broken and since when, a request, and a formal closing. Keep it short and factual, and always say when you are at home.

items.hr: Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren, ich wohne seit einem Jahr in der Wohnung im zweiten Stock.
items.en: Dear Sir or Madam, I have been living in the flat on the second floor for a year.
items.note: seit plus dative: seit einem Jahr. German uses the present tense here.

items.hr: Ich möchte Ihnen einen Schaden melden: Die Heizung im Wohnzimmer wird nicht mehr warm.
items.en: I would like to report a defect to you: the heating in the living room no longer gets warm.
items.note: einen Schaden melden is the standard phrase for reporting a defect.

items.hr: Seit gestern tropft der Wasserhahn im Bad, und unter dem Waschbecken steht schon Wasser.
items.en: Since yesterday the tap in the bathroom has been dripping, and there is already water under the sink.
items.note: unter dem Waschbecken: dative, because it answers wo.

items.hr: Können Sie bitte einen Handwerker schicken? Am Freitag bin ich den ganzen Tag zu Hause.
items.en: Could you please send a repairman? On Friday I am at home all day.
items.note: Offering a time saves a whole exchange of messages.

items.hr: Vielen Dank im Voraus. Mit freundlichen Grüßen, Ihre Mieterin aus Wohnung 7.
items.en: Thank you in advance. Kind regards, your tenant from flat 7.
items.note: Mit freundlichen Grüßen is the standard formal closing, with ss written as ß after a long vowel.

activities.title: Die formelle Nachricht
activities.intro: Practise the fixed phrases of a formal written report and the preposition seit.

questions.prompt: Sie schreiben an die Hausverwaltung und kennen keinen Namen. Wie beginnen Sie?
questions.explanation: This is the standard formal opening when the name of the recipient is unknown. The other three are informal or belong at the end.

questions.prompt: ___ gestern tropft der Wasserhahn im Bad.
questions.explanation: seit marks a point in the past from which something is still going on, and it takes the dative.

questions.prompt: Am Ende einer formellen Nachricht schreibt man: Mit freundlichen ___.
questions.explanation: Grüßen is the dative plural of der Gruß. It keeps ß because the vowel u is long.

questions.prompt: Die Heizung ist kaputt, deshalb ___ ich der Hausverwaltung den Schaden.
questions.explanation: melden takes a dative person and an accusative thing: ich melde der Hausverwaltung den Schaden.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Could you please send a repairman?'
questions.explanation: In a question the modal verb comes first, and the infinitive schicken goes to the very end.

activities.title: Anruf bei der Hausverwaltung
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are phoning the property management about a dripping tap; keep the formal register and offer a time for the repair.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, Hausverwaltung. Was kann ich für Sie tun?
lines.en: Good afternoon, property management. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Tag, ich möchte einen Schaden melden. Ich wohne in der Wohnung im zweiten Stock.
lines.en: Good afternoon, I would like to report a defect. I live in the flat on the second floor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Um welchen Schaden geht es denn?
lines.en: What kind of defect is it about?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Seit gestern tropft der Wasserhahn im Bad, und unter dem Waschbecken steht schon Wasser.
lines.en: Since yesterday the tap in the bathroom has been dripping, and there is already water under the sink.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Das klingt dringend. Haben Sie das Wasser abgestellt?
lines.en: That sounds urgent. Have you turned the water off?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, das habe ich sofort gemacht. Können Sie bitte einen Handwerker schicken?
lines.en: Yes, I did that immediately. Could you please send a repairman?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wann sind Sie denn zu Hause?
lines.en: When are you at home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Am Freitag den ganzen Tag. Ich schreibe Ihnen alles noch einmal in einer E-Mail.
lines.en: On Friday all day. I will write everything to you once more in an email.

days.title: Über die eigene Wohnung sprechen
days.objective: Describe your own flat in detail, comparing it with your previous one using adjectives, comparatives and superlatives.
days.paretoFocus: Describing where you live is one of the most common small talk topics of all, and it lets you use every adjective ending and comparison form in one natural stretch of speech.
drills: Describe each room of your home in one sentence with an adjective before the noun.
Compare your current home with a previous one in three sentences using comparatives.
Name your favourite spot at home and say why, using weil.

items: Which case follows in when you say where you live?
How do you form the superlative before a noun?
How do you say which floor you live on?

activities.title: Meine Wohnung beschreiben
activities.intro: To describe where you live, combine three things you already know: in plus dative for location, adjective endings before every noun, and comparatives and superlatives to compare flats. This turns a bare list of rooms into a real description.

items.hr: Ich wohne in einer hellen Zweizimmerwohnung im dritten Stock.
items.en: I live in a bright two room flat on the third floor.
items.note: in einer hellen Wohnung: dative feminine, adjective ending -en.

items.hr: Das Wohnzimmer ist der schönste Raum, weil es zwei große Fenster hat.
items.en: The living room is the nicest room, because it has two big windows.
items.note: Superlative before a noun: der schönste Raum, die schönste Wohnung.

items.hr: Meine neue Wohnung ist kleiner als die alte, aber sie liegt viel ruhiger.
items.en: My new flat is smaller than the old one, but it is in a much quieter spot.
items.note: kleiner als, comparative plus als.

items.hr: Vom Balkon aus sieht man einen kleinen Park und viele alte Bäume.
items.en: From the balcony you can see a small park and lots of old trees.
items.note: einen kleinen Park: masculine accusative after einen.

items.hr: Am liebsten sitze ich abends am Küchentisch, weil dort das beste Licht ist.
items.en: I like sitting at the kitchen table in the evening best, because the light is best there.
items.note: am liebsten is the superlative of gern, das beste Licht before a noun.

activities.title: So wohne ich
activities.intro: Practise the cases, adjective endings and comparison forms you need to describe a home.

questions.prompt: Ich wohne in ___ hellen Zweizimmerwohnung.
questions.explanation: wohnen answers wo, so in takes the dative, and the dative feminine of ein is einer.

questions.prompt: Das Wohnzimmer ist ___ Raum in der Wohnung.
questions.explanation: Before a noun the superlative needs an article and an ending: der schönste Raum. am schönsten is only used without a noun.

questions.prompt: Meine neue Wohnung ist ___ als die alte, denn sie hat nur zwei Zimmer.
questions.explanation: The comparative of klein adds -er and is followed by als, never by wie.

questions.prompt: Ich wohne im dritten ___ und fahre jeden Tag mit dem Aufzug nach oben.
questions.explanation: der Stock names the floor above the ground floor: im ersten Stock, im zweiten Stock, im dritten Stock.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'From the balcony you can see a small park.'
questions.explanation: von dem contracts to vom, the added aus means from there outwards, and einen kleinen Park is the accusative object.

activities.title: Zeig mal deine neue Wohnung
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Walking a friend through your new flat, you reach for adjectives before the nouns and compare it with your old one.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und, wie ist die neue Wohnung? Erzähl mal!
lines.en: So, what is the new flat like? Do tell!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sehr schön. Ich wohne jetzt in einer hellen Zweizimmerwohnung im dritten Stock.
lines.en: Very nice. I now live in a bright two room flat on the third floor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ist sie größer als die alte?
lines.en: Is it bigger than the old one?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nein, sie ist sogar kleiner, aber sie liegt viel ruhiger und hat einen großen Balkon.
lines.en: No, it is actually smaller, but it is in a much quieter spot and has a big balcony.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und welches Zimmer magst du am liebsten?
lines.en: And which room do you like best?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das Wohnzimmer ist der schönste Raum, weil es zwei große Fenster hat.
lines.en: The living room is the nicest room, because it has two big windows.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Klingt gemütlich. Was siehst du vom Balkon aus?
lines.en: Sounds cosy. What can you see from the balcony?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Einen kleinen Park und viele alte Bäume. Komm doch am Sonntag zum Kaffee vorbei!
lines.en: A small park and lots of old trees. Do come round for coffee on Sunday!

days.title: Die Anmeldung beim Bürgeramt
days.objective: You can tell a clerk at a registration office that you have moved and want to register your address, and name every document you must bring.
days.paretoFocus: Almost nothing else in Germany works without a registered address, so this one counter conversation unlocks the bank account, the health insurance and your official post.
drills: Say out loud, three times, the sentence you would use to open the conversation at the counter, then add when you moved in.
List in German the three things you take with you: the identity document, the landlord's confirmation and the completed form.
Explain to yourself in one English sentence what the registration certificate is for, then say its German name from memory.

items: How do you say 'I would like to register my address' using a reflexive verb?
Which document does the landlord give you, and which document do you receive from the office?
Which auxiliary verb does umziehen take in the perfect tense, and why?

activities.title: Ich möchte mich anmelden
activities.intro: When you move into a new home in Germany you must register your address at the local registration office within a legal deadline, usually about two weeks. The verb is reflexive: sich anmelden, so you always need mich, sich or uns. You bring an identity document, the written confirmation from your landlord and the filled-in form, and you leave with a registration certificate that you will need again and again.

items.hr: Guten Tag, ich möchte mich anmelden.
items.en: Good afternoon, I would like to register my address.
items.note: sich anmelden is reflexive: ich melde mich an, ich möchte mich anmelden. Use Sie with any official, never du.

items.hr: Ich bin vor einer Woche umgezogen und muss mich innerhalb der Frist anmelden.
items.en: I moved a week ago and have to register within the deadline.
items.note: umziehen takes sein in the perfect: ich bin umgezogen. die Frist is the legal deadline, roughly two weeks after moving in.

items.hr: Ich habe die Wohnungsgeberbestätigung von meinem Vermieter dabei.
items.en: I have the landlord's confirmation of residence with me.
items.note: die Wohnungsgeberbestätigung is the written statement from der Vermieter that you really live in the flat.

items.hr: Brauchen Sie meinen Personalausweis oder meinen Reisepass?
items.en: Do you need my identity card or my passport?
items.note: der Personalausweis, der Reisepass. After brauchen both stand in the accusative: meinen.

items.hr: Bekomme ich die Meldebescheinigung heute?
items.en: Do I get the registration certificate today?
items.note: die Meldebescheinigung is the proof of registration. Keep it: the bank, the insurer and many employers ask for it.

activities.title: Am Schalter im Bürgeramt
activities.intro: Practise the reflexive verb and the documents you need for registering an address.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says at the counter that you want to register your address?
questions.explanation: sich anmelden needs the accusative reflexive pronoun mich, and with möchte the infinitive anmelden goes to the very end of the sentence.

questions.prompt: You have just moved in. Which paper does your landlord have to give you for the registration?
questions.explanation: Der Vermieter writes die Wohnungsgeberbestätigung. Die Meldebescheinigung is what the office gives you afterwards, so it comes at the end, not the beginning.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct reflexive pronoun: 'Ich melde ___ heute an.'
questions.explanation: In the first person singular the accusative reflexive pronoun is mich. The separable prefix an stays at the end of the main clause.

questions.prompt: Complete the perfect tense with the right auxiliary verb: 'Ich ___ letzten Monat umgezogen.'
questions.explanation: umziehen describes a change of place, so it forms the perfect with sein: ich bin umgezogen, not ich habe umgezogen.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I have my passport and the form with me.'
questions.explanation: The conjugated verb habe stands in second position, and the little word dabei behaves like a separable prefix and closes the sentence.

activities.title: Die neue Adresse anmelden
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You have moved into a new flat and are at the counter of the local registration office, using sich anmelden and naming your documents.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, was kann ich für Sie tun?
lines.en: Good afternoon, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Tag, ich möchte mich anmelden. Ich bin vor zehn Tagen umgezogen.
lines.en: Good afternoon, I would like to register my address. I moved ten days ago.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Haben Sie das Formular und die Wohnungsgeberbestätigung dabei?
lines.en: Do you have the form and the landlord's confirmation with you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, hier sind beide. Brauchen Sie auch meinen Personalausweis?
lines.en: Yes, here are both. Do you also need my identity card?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja, bitte. Ihre neue Wohnung ist also in dieser Straße?
lines.en: Yes, please. So your new flat is in this street?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Genau. Bekomme ich die Meldebescheinigung sofort?
lines.en: Exactly. Do I get the registration certificate straight away?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sie bekommen sie gleich mit. Bewahren Sie das Papier gut auf, Sie brauchen es noch oft.
lines.en: You get it right away. Keep the document safe, you will need it often.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das mache ich. Vielen Dank für Ihre Hilfe.
lines.en: I will. Thank you very much for your help.

days.title: Einen Termin vereinbaren und verschieben
days.objective: You can arrange an appointment at an office or practice, react to the times you are offered, and move or cancel an appointment politely.
days.paretoFocus: Offices, doctors and advisers in Germany work almost entirely by appointment, so arranging and moving one is the single most repeated transaction of your week.
drills: Say three different sentences offering a day and a time, using passen with the dative: mir, Ihnen.
Imagine your appointment is tomorrow and you are ill. Say aloud that you cannot come and want to move it to next week.
Practise the difference between moving an appointment and cancelling it in two spoken German sentences.

items: Which verb do you use for arranging an appointment, and which for moving it?
Which case does passen take, and how would you ask a stranger if a time suits them?
Which preposition follows verschieben when you name the new time?

activities.title: Einen Termin vereinbaren
activities.intro: Three verbs cover almost every appointment: einen Termin vereinbaren to arrange one, verschieben to move it, absagen to cancel it. The verb passen works with the dative, so you say Der Termin passt mir gut. Keep to Sie in every one of these calls and visits, because you are speaking to an adult stranger in a professional role.

items.hr: Guten Tag, ich möchte einen Termin vereinbaren.
items.en: Good afternoon, I would like to arrange an appointment.
items.note: der Termin. vereinbaren is the neutral, official verb; ausmachen is the more casual everyday alternative.

items.hr: Haben Sie am Montagvormittag noch etwas frei?
items.en: Do you still have anything free on Monday morning?
items.note: der Montagvormittag. am + day part: am Montagvormittag, am Nachmittag, am Abend.

items.hr: Passt Ihnen der Dienstag um zehn Uhr?
items.en: Does Tuesday at ten o'clock suit you?
items.note: passen takes the dative: Das passt mir, das passt Ihnen. The person suited stands in the dative, not the accusative.

items.hr: Leider kann ich den Termin nicht wahrnehmen.
items.en: Unfortunately I cannot keep the appointment.
items.note: einen Termin wahrnehmen is the formal way to say you attend it. wahrnehmen is separable: ich nehme den Termin wahr.

items.hr: Können wir den Termin auf nächste Woche verschieben?
items.en: Can we move the appointment to next week?
items.note: verschieben auf + accusative for the new date. absagen means to cancel with no new date, so use it only when you really mean it.

activities.title: Termine planen und ändern
activities.intro: Practise arranging, moving and cancelling appointments with the right verbs and cases.

questions.prompt: Which verb goes with 'einen Termin' when you want to arrange a new one?
questions.explanation: einen Termin vereinbaren is the fixed collocation for arranging. verschieben moves an existing one and absagen cancels it.

questions.prompt: Your appointment is on Tuesday but you cannot come. You still want it, only at another time. What do you say?
questions.explanation: verschieben keeps the appointment and only changes the time. absagen would delete it completely and you would have to start again.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct dative pronoun for a formal 'you': 'Passt ___ der Donnerstag um neun Uhr?'
questions.explanation: passen takes the dative, and the polite Sie becomes Ihnen in the dative. It keeps its capital letter as a form of address.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: 'Können wir den Termin ___ nächste Woche verschieben?'
questions.explanation: verschieben names the new point in time with auf plus the accusative: auf nächste Woche, auf Freitag, auf den Nachmittag.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I would like to arrange an appointment for next Monday.'
questions.explanation: möchte takes second position and pushes the infinitive vereinbaren to the end. für governs the accusative, so der Montag becomes nächsten Montag.

activities.title: Der Termin passt nicht mehr
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call an office to move an appointment you can no longer keep, using verschieben and passen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Morgen, Sie sprechen mit der Terminvergabe.
lines.en: Good morning, you are speaking to appointment scheduling.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Morgen. Ich habe morgen einen Termin, aber leider kann ich ihn nicht wahrnehmen.
lines.en: Good morning. I have an appointment tomorrow, but unfortunately I cannot keep it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kein Problem. Möchten Sie den Termin absagen oder verschieben?
lines.en: No problem. Would you like to cancel the appointment or move it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Verschieben, bitte. Haben Sie nächste Woche noch etwas frei?
lines.en: Move it, please. Do you still have anything free next week?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Am Mittwoch um elf Uhr. Passt Ihnen das?
lines.en: On Wednesday at eleven o'clock. Does that suit you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Der Mittwoch passt mir sehr gut. Muss ich wieder alle Papiere mitbringen?
lines.en: Wednesday suits me very well. Do I have to bring all the documents again?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja, bringen Sie alles noch einmal mit. Ihr Termin ist notiert.
lines.en: Yes, bring everything again. Your appointment is noted.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Danke, dass es so schnell geht. Bis Mittwoch.
lines.en: Thank you that it works so quickly. See you on Wednesday.

days.title: Ein Formular ausfüllen
days.objective: You can read the standard field names on a German official form, fill them in correctly and sign in the right place.
days.paretoFocus: The same twenty field names come back on every registration, bank and insurance form, so learning them once saves you hesitation at every counter for years.
drills: Write out your own details under the German field names for surname, first name, date of birth and address.
Say aloud what happens to a form that has no signature, using the word ungültig.
Cover the English column of the field names and name five of them from memory in German with their articles.

items: Which German word means surname and which one means first name?
Where do the separable prefix aus and the inseparable verb unterschreiben stand in a sentence?
What does a clerk mean when they ask you to write in Druckbuchstaben?

activities.title: Die Felder im Formular
activities.intro: German forms use a small, fixed set of field names, and they hardly ever change from office to office. Two verbs matter: ausfüllen, which is separable, and unterschreiben, which is inseparable and never splits. A form without a signature is treated as if it had never been handed in.

items.hr: Bitte füllen Sie das Formular in Druckbuchstaben aus.
items.en: Please fill in the form in block letters.
items.note: das Formular. ausfüllen is separable, so the prefix aus closes the sentence: Sie füllen das Formular aus.

items.hr: Der Familienname steht oben, der Vorname direkt darunter.
items.en: The surname is at the top, the first name directly below it.
items.note: der Familienname, der Vorname. On many forms you will also see der Nachname, which means the same as der Familienname.

items.hr: Bei Geburtsdatum schreibe ich Tag, Monat und Jahr.
items.en: Under date of birth I write day, month and year.
items.note: das Geburtsdatum, der Geburtsort. German dates run from the smallest unit to the largest: 03.11.1990.

items.hr: Das Feld Staatsangehörigkeit habe ich leer gelassen.
items.en: I left the nationality field empty.
items.note: das Feld, die Staatsangehörigkeit. leer lassen means to leave blank, and a clerk will usually ask you to complete it.

items.hr: Unten müssen Sie noch unterschreiben, sonst ist das Formular ungültig.
items.en: You still have to sign at the bottom, otherwise the form is invalid.
items.note: unterschreiben is inseparable: ich unterschreibe, ich habe unterschrieben. die Unterschrift is the signature itself.

activities.title: Felder und Unterschrift
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of official forms and the two verbs that describe what you do with them.

questions.prompt: Which German field name asks for your surname?
questions.explanation: der Familienname is the family name. der Vorname is the given name, der Geburtsort the place of birth and der Wohnort the place where you live now.

questions.prompt: The clerk says 'Bitte unterschreiben Sie unten.' What are you being asked to do?
options: Write your signature at the bottom of the page.
Write your name in capitals at the top.
Tick the box on the left.
Add a photograph to the form.
questions.answer: Write your signature at the bottom of the page.
questions.explanation: unterschreiben means to sign, and unten places it at the bottom of the sheet. The noun for the signature itself is die Unterschrift.

questions.prompt: Complete the separable verb: 'Ich fülle das Formular gleich ___.'
questions.explanation: In a main clause the separable prefix leaves the verb and closes the sentence, so füllen keeps its position and aus goes last.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct definite article: 'Bitte schreiben Sie ___ Geburtsdatum in dieses Feld.'
questions.explanation: Datum is neuter, so it takes das, and the accusative neuter article after schreiben is still das.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Please fill in the form in block letters.'
questions.explanation: In a polite Sie imperative the verb comes first after bitte, the subject Sie follows it, and the separable prefix aus stands at the very end.

activities.title: Hilfe beim Formular
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A clerk hands you a form and checks it with you, so you use the field names and the verbs ausfüllen and unterschreiben.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hier ist das Formular. Bitte füllen Sie es in Druckbuchstaben aus.
lines.en: Here is the form. Please fill it in in block letters.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gern. Kommt der Familienname in das erste Feld?
lines.en: Gladly. Does the surname go in the first field?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja, und der Vorname direkt darunter. Das Geburtsdatum schreiben Sie mit Punkten.
lines.en: Yes, and the first name directly below it. Write the date of birth with full stops.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das Feld Staatsangehörigkeit habe ich leer gelassen. Ist das ein Problem?
lines.en: I left the nationality field empty. Is that a problem?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja, dieses Feld muss ausgefüllt sein. Sonst kann ich das Formular nicht annehmen.
lines.en: Yes, that field has to be completed. Otherwise I cannot accept the form.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut, ich ergänze es sofort. Muss ich auch unten unterschreiben?
lines.en: All right, I will add it straight away. Do I also have to sign at the bottom?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Unbedingt. Ohne Unterschrift ist das Formular ungültig.
lines.en: Definitely. Without a signature the form is invalid.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fertig. Bitte schön, hier ist alles.
lines.en: Done. Here you are, everything is there.

days.title: Nachfragen, wenn man etwas nicht versteht
days.objective: You can stop a fast or unclear speaker at any moment and ask them to repeat, slow down, explain a word or spell it out.
days.paretoFocus: This is the survival skill that makes every other lesson usable, because you will always meet words you do not know and you are allowed to interrupt and ask.
drills: Say the four core rescue sentences out loud until you can produce any of them within one second.
Take any German word you met this week and ask its meaning aloud, then ask how it is spelled.
Practise interrupting mid-sentence with Entschuldigung, so that starting the interruption feels normal.

items: How do you ask someone to say the same thing again?
Which question asks what a word means, and which one asks for its spelling?
What is the polite word you use to interrupt before you ask anything?

activities.title: Das habe ich nicht verstanden
activities.intro: Nobody expects you to understand every word, and clerks, doctors and shop staff repeat things all day long. The important thing is to interrupt early instead of nodding and hoping. Start with Entschuldigung, then ask directly, and keep to Sie because you are speaking to an adult stranger.

items.hr: Entschuldigung, das habe ich nicht verstanden.
items.en: Excuse me, I did not understand that.
items.note: verstehen has the irregular participle verstanden with no ge, because ver is an inseparable prefix.

items.hr: Können Sie das bitte wiederholen?
items.en: Can you repeat that, please?
items.note: wiederholen is inseparable here: ich wiederhole. Noch einmal, bitte works as a short version.

items.hr: Können Sie bitte etwas langsamer sprechen?
items.en: Can you please speak a little more slowly?
items.note: langsamer is the comparative of langsam. etwas softens it to a little, which sounds friendlier than a bare command.

items.hr: Was bedeutet das Wort Meldebescheinigung?
items.en: What does the word Meldebescheinigung mean?
items.note: das Wort. Was bedeutet ...? asks for meaning; Wie heißt das auf Deutsch? asks for a word you already know in your language.

items.hr: Wie schreibt man das, bitte?
items.en: How do you spell that, please?
items.note: man is the impersonal subject, used where English says you in general. Add Buchstabieren Sie das bitte if you want it letter by letter.

activities.title: Nachfragen und klären
activities.intro: Practise the sentences that let you interrupt and ask for help understanding.

questions.prompt: The official is speaking very fast and you are losing the thread. What do you say?
options: Können Sie bitte etwas langsamer sprechen?
Können Sie bitte etwas schneller sprechen?
questions.answer: Können Sie bitte etwas langsamer sprechen?
questions.explanation: langsamer is the comparative of langsam and is exactly what you need. schneller would ask for the opposite and make things worse.

questions.prompt: You hear a word you have never met and want to know what it means. Which question do you use?
questions.explanation: bedeuten asks for meaning. Wie schreibt man das? would only get you the spelling of a word you still do not understand.

questions.prompt: Complete the perfect tense: 'Entschuldigung, das habe ich leider nicht ___.'
questions.explanation: The participle of verstehen is verstanden. Inseparable prefixes such as ver block the ge, so there is no geverstanden.

questions.prompt: Complete the request so that it asks for the same information a second time: 'Können Sie das bitte ___?'
questions.explanation: After the modal können the second verb stands as an infinitive at the end of the sentence.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'How do you spell that, please?'
questions.explanation: In a question with a question word the verb comes second and the subject man follows it directly.

activities.title: Bitte noch einmal
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An official explains something too quickly and you interrupt several times until you really understand.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sie brauchen dafür noch die Bescheinigung Ihres Wohnungsgebers und einen Nachweis über Ihr Einkommen.
lines.en: For that you still need the confirmation from your landlord and proof of your income.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Entschuldigung, das habe ich nicht verstanden. Können Sie das bitte wiederholen?
lines.en: Excuse me, I did not understand that. Can you repeat it, please?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Natürlich. Sie brauchen eine Bescheinigung und einen Einkommensnachweis.
lines.en: Of course. You need a confirmation and proof of income.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Was bedeutet Einkommensnachweis genau?
lines.en: What exactly does Einkommensnachweis mean?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ein Papier, das zeigt, wie viel Geld Sie im Monat verdienen.
lines.en: A document that shows how much money you earn per month.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jetzt ist es klar. Können Sie bitte etwas langsamer sprechen? Ich schreibe alles mit.
lines.en: Now it is clear. Can you please speak a little more slowly? I am writing everything down.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gern. Sagen Sie einfach Bescheid, wenn ich zu schnell bin.
lines.en: Gladly. Just say so if I am too fast.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Danke. Und wie schreibt man das erste Wort?
lines.en: Thank you. And how do you spell the first word?

days.title: Ein Konto eröffnen
days.objective: You can open an everyday current account, ask about fees and card, and name the documents the bank staff will want to see.
days.paretoFocus: Rent, salary and almost every contract in Germany run through a current account, so opening one correctly is the second door after registering your address.
drills: Say aloud which two documents you take to the appointment for opening an account.
Ask three questions about costs using Wie hoch sind ...? with different nouns.
Explain to yourself in German what an account number is called and where you can read it.

items: Which German word names the everyday account for salary and bills?
How do you ask how high the fees are?
Which case does auf take in 'auf dem Kontoauszug', and why?

activities.title: Ein Girokonto eröffnen
activities.intro: The everyday account in Germany is das Girokonto, and it is what your salary arrives in and your rent leaves from. To open one you normally need an identity document and your registration certificate. Ask about fees before you sign, because accounts differ a lot in what they cost.

items.hr: Guten Tag, ich möchte ein Girokonto eröffnen.
items.en: Good afternoon, I would like to open a current account.
items.note: das Girokonto is the everyday account; das Sparkonto is a savings account and cannot be used for daily payments.

items.hr: Dafür brauchen Sie einen Ausweis und eine Meldebescheinigung.
items.en: For that you need an identity document and a registration certificate.
items.note: der Ausweis is the general word for an identity document. This is exactly where your registration from the first lesson of this block pays off.

items.hr: Bekomme ich eine Bankkarte zu dem Konto?
items.en: Do I get a bank card with the account?
items.note: die Bankkarte. zu takes the dative, so das Konto becomes dem Konto, often shortened to zum Konto.

items.hr: Wie hoch sind die Gebühren für das Konto?
items.en: How high are the fees for the account?
items.note: die Gebühr, plural die Gebühren. Wie hoch ...? is the standard German way to ask about amounts and prices in official contexts.

items.hr: Meine IBAN steht auf dem Kontoauszug.
items.en: My account number is on the account statement.
items.note: die IBAN is your account number in international form. der Kontoauszug is the statement listing what went in and out.

activities.title: In der Bank
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary and the questions you need when you open an account.

questions.prompt: Which account do you need for your salary, your rent and your daily payments?
questions.explanation: das Girokonto is the working everyday account. das Sparkonto only stores money, and the other two options are not accounts at all.

questions.prompt: Which question asks how much the account costs you per month?
questions.explanation: German asks about amounts with hoch, literally how high. wie lange asks about time and wie oft about frequency.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct indefinite article: 'Ich möchte ___ Konto eröffnen.'
questions.explanation: Konto is neuter, and the neuter accusative of the indefinite article is identical to the nominative, so it stays ein.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition and article together: 'Meine IBAN steht ___ Kontoauszug.'
questions.explanation: stehen describes a position, not a movement, so auf takes the dative here, and der Kontoauszug becomes dem Kontoauszug.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'For that you need an identity document and a registration certificate.'
questions.explanation: Dafür opens the sentence, so the verb brauchen takes second place and the subject Sie follows it. brauchen governs the accusative: einen Ausweis, eine Meldebescheinigung.

activities.title: Der Termin in der Bank
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are at a bank counter to open a current account and you ask about documents, card and fees.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, Sie haben einen Termin zur Kontoeröffnung?
lines.en: Good afternoon, you have an appointment to open an account?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, genau. Ich möchte ein Girokonto eröffnen.
lines.en: Yes, exactly. I would like to open a current account.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Haben Sie einen Ausweis und eine Meldebescheinigung dabei?
lines.en: Do you have an identity document and a registration certificate with you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Beides habe ich hier. Wie hoch sind die Gebühren für das Konto?
lines.en: I have both here. How high are the fees for the account?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Es gibt eine kleine Gebühr pro Monat. Alle Überweisungen sind kostenlos.
lines.en: There is a small fee per month. All transfers are free of charge.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das ist in Ordnung. Bekomme ich auch eine Bankkarte?
lines.en: That is all right. Do I also get a bank card?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Die Karte kommt in etwa einer Woche mit der Post.
lines.en: The card will arrive by post in about a week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Und wo finde ich meine IBAN? Auf dem Kontoauszug?
lines.en: And where do I find my account number? On the account statement?

days.title: Überweisung, Lastschrift und Dauerauftrag
days.objective: You can tell the three German payment types apart and say which one you want for rent, electricity and a one-off payment.
days.paretoFocus: Choosing the wrong payment type means unpaid rent or money leaving your account twice, so this vocabulary protects you from expensive mistakes.
drills: Say in German which payment type you would use for rent, and explain in one sentence why.
Describe out loud what happens with a direct debit: who takes the money, and from whose account.
Practise the perfect tense of überweisen in three sentences with different amounts.

items: What is the difference between a standing order and a direct debit?
Which information do you need about the recipient before you can send a transfer?
What is the participle of überweisen, and why does it have no ge?

activities.title: Geld überweisen und abbuchen lassen
activities.intro: German banking runs on three ideas. In einer Überweisung you push money out yourself, in einem Dauerauftrag the bank repeats the same amount for you automatically, and bei einer Lastschrift the other party pulls the money from your account. Rent is usually a standing order, electricity and phone are usually direct debits.

items.hr: Ich möchte Geld auf ein anderes Konto überweisen.
items.en: I would like to transfer money to another account.
items.note: überweisen is inseparable: ich überweise, ich habe überwiesen. The noun is die Überweisung.

items.hr: Für die Überweisung brauche ich die IBAN des Empfängers.
items.en: For the transfer I need the recipient's account number.
items.note: der Empfänger, genitive des Empfängers. Without the correct IBAN the money cannot be sent.

items.hr: Die Miete zahle ich jeden Monat mit einem Dauerauftrag.
items.en: I pay the rent every month with a standing order.
items.note: der Dauerauftrag: the same fixed amount goes out on the same day, and you control it.

items.hr: Den Strom bezahle ich per Lastschrift, der Anbieter zieht das Geld selbst ein.
items.en: I pay for electricity by direct debit, the provider collects the money itself.
items.note: die Lastschrift: the other side pulls the money, and the amount can change. einziehen is separable: er zieht das Geld ein.

items.hr: Auf dem Kontoauszug sehe ich jede Buchung.
items.en: On the account statement I see every transaction.
items.note: die Buchung is one entry on the statement. Check it regularly so a wrong direct debit does not go unnoticed.

activities.title: Welche Zahlung passt?
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right payment type and using the verb überweisen correctly.

questions.prompt: The same amount leaves your account on the same day every month because you set it up yourself. What is that called?
questions.explanation: A Dauerauftrag repeats a fixed amount that you have ordered. A Lastschrift is set up by the other party and the amount can vary.

questions.prompt: The electricity provider takes the money from your account itself, and the amount changes each time. Which word describes this?
questions.explanation: With a Lastschrift you give permission and the other side collects. That is why variable bills such as electricity almost always work this way.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing preposition: 'Ich überweise das Geld ___ das Konto meiner Vermieterin.'
questions.explanation: Money moves onto an account, so German uses auf with the accusative here to show the direction of the movement.

questions.prompt: Complete the perfect tense with the correct participle: 'Ich habe die Miete gestern ___.'
questions.explanation: The prefix über is inseparable in this verb, so the participle takes no ge: überweisen becomes überwiesen.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I pay the rent every month with a standing order.'
questions.explanation: Die Miete stands first as the topic, so the verb zahle takes second position and the subject ich follows. mit always takes the dative: mit einem Dauerauftrag.

activities.title: Miete und Strom bezahlen
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A bank adviser helps you set up your regular payments, so you use Überweisung, Dauerauftrag and Lastschrift.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ihr Konto ist jetzt aktiv. Möchten Sie regelmäßige Zahlungen einrichten?
lines.en: Your account is active now. Would you like to set up regular payments?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, bitte. Die Miete ist jeden Monat gleich hoch.
lines.en: Yes, please. The rent is the same amount every month.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dann nehmen wir einen Dauerauftrag. Ich brauche die IBAN des Empfängers.
lines.en: Then we will use a standing order. I need the recipient's account number.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hier steht sie im Mietvertrag. Und wie bezahle ich den Strom?
lines.en: Here it is in the rental contract. And how do I pay for the electricity?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dort ist die Summe nicht immer gleich, also besser per Lastschrift.
lines.en: There the amount is not always the same, so a direct debit is better.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das heißt, der Anbieter zieht das Geld selbst ein?
lines.en: That means the provider collects the money itself?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Genau. Prüfen Sie jede Buchung auf dem Kontoauszug.
lines.en: Exactly. Check every transaction on the account statement.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mache ich. Eine einzelne Rechnung überweise ich dann einfach selbst.
lines.en: I will. A single invoice I will then simply transfer myself.

days.title: Die Krankenversicherung verstehen
days.objective: You can explain that health insurance is compulsory, say whether you are in the statutory or the private system, and handle the card and the small extra payment at a practice.
days.paretoFocus: Health insurance is legally required and touches your salary, your doctor visits and your medicines, so understanding four words here prevents months of confusion.
drills: Say in German that everyone in Germany must be insured, using the noun for obligation.
Name the card you show at a practice, and say the sentence you would use when handing it over.
Explain aloud in one German sentence what the small extra payment for medicines is called.

items: What are the two systems of health insurance called in German?
What do you hand over at the reception of a doctor's practice?
What is the difference between beim Arzt and zum Arzt?

activities.title: Gesetzlich oder privat versichert
activities.intro: In Germany health insurance is not optional: everyone must be insured. Most people are in the statutory system, a minority in the private one, and if you are employed your contribution is taken from your pay automatically. At a practice you hand over your insurance card, and for some medicines you pay a small share yourself.

items.hr: In Deutschland ist die Krankenversicherung Pflicht.
items.en: In Germany health insurance is compulsory.
items.note: die Krankenversicherung, die Pflicht. Nobody may live in Germany without health insurance.

items.hr: Die meisten Menschen sind gesetzlich versichert, wenige privat.
items.en: Most people are insured in the statutory system, few privately.
items.note: gesetzlich means statutory, privat means private. versichert sein is the fixed way to say you have cover.

items.hr: In der Praxis zeige ich immer meine Versichertenkarte.
items.en: At the practice I always show my insurance card.
items.note: die Versichertenkarte, die Praxis. Without the card the practice may ask you to pay yourself first.

items.hr: Für manche Medikamente muss man eine Zuzahlung leisten.
items.en: For some medicines you have to pay a small share yourself.
items.note: die Zuzahlung is the part of the price the insurance does not cover. eine Zuzahlung leisten is the standard collocation.

items.hr: Der Beitrag wird direkt vom Gehalt abgezogen.
items.en: The contribution is deducted directly from the salary.
items.note: der Beitrag, das Gehalt. abziehen is separable: man zieht den Beitrag ab.

activities.title: Versichert sein
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary of the German health insurance system and the prepositions that go with a doctor's visit.

questions.prompt: How would you describe health insurance for people living in Germany?
options: Sie ist nur für Kinder nötig.
Sie ist nur im Winter nötig.
questions.explanation: Health insurance is legally compulsory. The choice is only between the statutory and the private system, never between having cover and having none.

questions.prompt: What do you hand over at the reception of a doctor's practice?
questions.explanation: die Versichertenkarte proves that your insurance will pay. The other three documents belong to housing and banking, not to health care.

questions.prompt: Complete with the adjective that is the opposite of privat in this system: 'Ich bin ___ versichert.'
questions.explanation: The two systems are called gesetzlich and privat, so the opposite of privat versichert is gesetzlich versichert.

questions.prompt: Complete with the merged preposition and article that shows movement: 'Ich gehe morgen ___ Arzt.'
questions.explanation: Movement towards a person uses zu plus the dative, and zu dem contracts to zum. Use beim Arzt only when you are already there.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'For some medicines you have to pay a small share yourself.'
questions.explanation: The phrase with für opens the sentence, so muss stands second and the impersonal subject man follows. The infinitive leisten closes the sentence.

activities.title: Fragen zur Versicherung
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are at the counter of a health insurer asking how the system works for you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, wie kann ich Ihnen helfen?
lines.en: Good afternoon, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich fange nächsten Monat an zu arbeiten und brauche eine Krankenversicherung.
lines.en: I start working next month and need health insurance.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dann sind Sie gesetzlich versichert. Der Beitrag wird vom Gehalt abgezogen.
lines.en: Then you are in the statutory system. The contribution is deducted from your salary.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muss ich den Beitrag selbst überweisen?
lines.en: Do I have to transfer the contribution myself?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nein, das macht Ihr Arbeitgeber. Sie bekommen bald Ihre Versichertenkarte.
lines.en: No, your employer does that. You will soon receive your insurance card.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Und wenn ich zum Arzt gehe, ist dann alles bezahlt?
lines.en: And if I go to the doctor, is everything paid for then?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fast alles. Für manche Medikamente zahlen Sie eine kleine Zuzahlung.
lines.en: Almost everything. For some medicines you pay a small extra share.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut, das habe ich verstanden. Vielen Dank für die Erklärung.
lines.en: Good, I have understood that. Thank you very much for the explanation.

days.title: Ein Brief von der Behörde, was tun
days.objective: You can work calmly through an official letter: find the deadline, note the reference number, answer in writing and ask for help in time.
days.paretoFocus: Official letters look frightening but follow one fixed pattern, and knowing the pattern turns panic into a five minute routine.
drills: Take any letter you have received and say aloud, in German, where the deadline and the reference number are.
Say in German why you prefer to answer in writing, using denn and the word for proof.
Practise one sentence asking a colleague or neighbour for help with a letter, using um Hilfe bitten.

items: What is die Frist and what happens if you ignore it?
Which number must you always quote in your reply, and where do you find it?
How do you say that you would rather answer in writing than by phone?

activities.title: Der Brief von der Behörde
activities.intro: An official German letter always contains the same three things: a deadline, a reference number and a request. Read them in that order and the letter loses most of its power to frighten you. Answer in writing so that you have proof, and if the deadline is short, ask for help immediately instead of waiting.

items.hr: Heute ist ein Brief von der Behörde gekommen.
items.en: A letter from the public authority arrived today.
items.note: die Behörde is any public authority or office. kommen forms the perfect with sein: ist gekommen.

items.hr: Zuerst suche ich die Frist im Brief.
items.en: First I look for the deadline in the letter.
items.note: die Frist is the last day for your answer. A missed Frist can cost money, so look for it before anything else.

items.hr: Oben rechts steht das Aktenzeichen, das muss ich immer angeben.
items.en: The reference number is at the top right, I always have to quote it.
items.note: das Aktenzeichen identifies your file. angeben is separable: ich gebe das Aktenzeichen an.

items.hr: Ich antworte immer schriftlich, denn so habe ich einen Nachweis.
items.en: I always answer in writing, because that way I have proof.
items.note: schriftlich means in writing, der Nachweis means proof. denn is a simple connector and does not change the word order.

items.hr: Wenn die Frist kurz ist, bitte ich sofort um Hilfe.
items.en: If the deadline is short, I ask for help immediately.
items.note: um Hilfe bitten takes um plus the accusative. After the wenn clause the main verb comes first: bitte ich.

activities.title: Ruhig auf Post reagieren
activities.intro: Practise the vocabulary and the calm procedure for dealing with an official letter.

questions.prompt: What does 'die Frist' tell you in an official letter?
options: den letzten Tag für Ihre Antwort
questions.answer: den letzten Tag für Ihre Antwort
questions.explanation: die Frist is the deadline, the last day on which your answer still counts as being on time.

questions.prompt: Which number do you have to quote when you write your reply?
questions.explanation: das Aktenzeichen identifies your file among thousands of others. Without it your reply can take much longer to reach the right desk.

questions.prompt: Complete with the adverb that means in written form: 'Ich antworte lieber ___ als am Telefon.'
questions.explanation: schriftlich is the adverb for in writing, and it contrasts here with mündlich, which means spoken.

questions.prompt: Complete the fixed expression with the correct noun: 'Wenn ich unsicher bin, bitte ich um ___.'
questions.explanation: um Hilfe bitten is a fixed phrase, and like every German noun Hilfe keeps its capital letter.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'First I look for the deadline in the letter.'
questions.explanation: Zuerst takes first position, so the verb suche moves to second place and the subject ich comes after it. in dem contracts to im.

activities.title: Was steht in dem Brief?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You show an official letter to an experienced neighbour and work through it step by step, using Frist, Aktenzeichen and schriftlich.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sie sehen besorgt aus. Ist etwas mit dem Brief?
lines.en: You look worried. Is something wrong with the letter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Er ist von einer Behörde und ich verstehe nur die Hälfte.
lines.en: It is from a public authority and I only understand half of it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ruhig. Schauen Sie zuerst, welche Frist dort steht.
lines.en: Calm down. First look at what deadline is given there.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hier: Ich muss bis zum Ende des Monats antworten.
lines.en: Here: I have to answer by the end of the month.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Das ist genug Zeit. Notieren Sie das Aktenzeichen oben rechts.
lines.en: That is enough time. Note down the reference number at the top right.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Habe ich. Soll ich anrufen oder schriftlich antworten?
lines.en: I have. Should I call or answer in writing?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Schreiben Sie, denn dann haben Sie einen Nachweis.
lines.en: Write, because then you have proof.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut. Und wenn ich etwas nicht verstehe, bitte ich um Hilfe.
lines.en: Good. And if I do not understand something, I will ask for help.

days.title: Höflich bitten mit würde und könnte
days.objective: You can turn any direct request into an acceptable one at a counter by using könnten Sie and würden Sie with the verb at the end.
days.paretoFocus: The same request sounds rude or reasonable depending on one verb form, and this is the form that decides how helpful officials and staff are with you.
drills: Take three commands you might give and rebuild each one with könnten Sie and with würden Sie.
Say aloud the polite version of asking someone to repeat something and to speak more slowly.
Practise placing bitte correctly in five requests, just after the pronoun.

items: What is the difference in sound between Helfen Sie mir! and Könnten Sie mir helfen?
Where does the main verb stand in a request with würden Sie?
How do you distinguish könnten from konnten in writing and in meaning?

activities.title: Könnten Sie, würden Sie
activities.intro: German has a politeness gear, and this is it. Instead of the plain imperative you use könnten Sie or würden Sie plus an infinitive at the end of the sentence, and a request that sounded like an order suddenly sounds normal. At a counter or with any official this is not decoration: the direct imperative really does sound blunt.

items.hr: Könnten Sie das bitte noch einmal wiederholen?
items.en: Could you repeat that once more, please?
items.note: könnten is the polite form of können. The direct Wiederholen Sie das! sounds like an order at a counter.

items.hr: Würden Sie mir bitte helfen?
items.en: Would you help me, please?
items.note: würden plus an infinitive at the end makes almost any request polite. helfen takes the dative: mir, Ihnen.

items.hr: Ich hätte eine Frage.
items.en: I have a question.
items.note: hätte is the softened form of habe. Ich habe eine Frage is correct but sounds more demanding at a counter.

items.hr: Könnten Sie mir sagen, wo ich das Formular bekomme?
items.en: Could you tell me where I can get the form?
items.note: After sagen the indirect question sends its verb to the end: wo ich das Formular bekomme.

items.hr: Würden Sie bitte kurz warten? Ich hole meinen Ausweis.
items.en: Would you wait a moment, please? I will fetch my identity document.
items.note: bitte usually stands right after the pronoun or just before the key information, not at the very end.

activities.title: Bitten statt befehlen
activities.intro: Practise turning direct commands into polite requests with könnten and würden.

questions.prompt: Which request sounds acceptable to a clerk you have never met before?
questions.explanation: könnten plus bitte softens the request. The imperative sounds like an order, and Hilf uses du, which is a real mistake with an official.

questions.prompt: Which form is the polite request form of the verb können?
questions.explanation: The umlaut makes the difference: könnten is the polite request form, while konnten without the umlaut is the simple past and means were able to.

questions.prompt: Complete with the polite request form of werden: '___ Sie bitte das Fenster schließen?'
questions.explanation: würden plus an infinitive builds a polite request, and the infinitive schließen closes the sentence.

questions.prompt: Complete so that the sentence softly announces that you want to ask something: 'Ich ___ eine Frage.'
questions.explanation: hätte is the softened form of habe and is the normal way to open a question politely at a counter.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Could you please speak a little more slowly?'
questions.explanation: In a question the verb form Könnten comes first, the subject Sie follows, and the infinitive sprechen stands at the end.

activities.title: Eine höfliche Bitte am Schalter
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You need several things from a busy clerk and you ask for each of them with könnten Sie or würden Sie.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Der Nächste, bitte. Was brauchen Sie?
lines.en: Next, please. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Tag, ich hätte eine Frage. Könnten Sie mir sagen, wo ich das Formular bekomme?
lines.en: Good afternoon, I have a question. Could you tell me where I can get the form?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Die Formulare liegen dort hinten neben der Tür.
lines.en: The forms are over there at the back next to the door.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Würden Sie bitte kurz warten? Ich hole eins.
lines.en: Would you wait a moment, please? I will fetch one.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kein Problem, ich bin noch da.
lines.en: No problem, I am still here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Danke. Könnten Sie das mit dem Aktenzeichen bitte noch einmal wiederholen?
lines.en: Thank you. Could you please repeat the part about the reference number?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gern. Sie tragen es oben in das erste Feld ein.
lines.en: Gladly. You enter it at the top in the first field.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jetzt ist alles klar. Würden Sie bitte kurz prüfen, ob das so stimmt?
lines.en: Now everything is clear. Would you please check briefly whether this is correct?

days.title: Konjunktiv II, hätte und wäre
days.objective: You can use hätte and wäre to order, request, thank and refuse politely, and recognise them as the softened forms of haben and sein.
days.paretoFocus: hätte and wäre appear in almost every polite German exchange, from a bakery counter to an office, so these two short forms carry an enormous amount of daily usage.
drills: Say five things you want using ich hätte gern, changing only the object each time.
Practise the full set aloud: ich hätte, Sie hätten, ich wäre, Sie wären.
Turn one direct refusal into a soft one using wäre leider nicht möglich.

items: Which two verbs do hätte and wäre come from?
What is the difference in effect between Haben Sie noch einen Termin? and Hätten Sie noch einen Termin?
How do you thank someone in advance for a favour they have not done yet?

activities.title: Ich hätte gern, das wäre nett
activities.intro: hätte and wäre soften haben and sein, and they are the most used politeness forms in German. The pattern is short: ich hätte, du hättest, er hätte, wir hätten, Sie hätten, and ich wäre, du wärest, er wäre, wir wären, Sie wären. They soften what you want, what you ask for and even what you have to refuse.

items.hr: Ich hätte gern einen Termin am Vormittag.
items.en: I would like an appointment in the morning.
items.note: Ich hätte gern is the standard polite way to say what you want, in an office and in a shop alike.

items.hr: Hätten Sie vielleicht noch einen Termin am Freitag?
items.en: Would you perhaps still have an appointment on Friday?
items.note: Hätten Sie ...? leaves the other person room to say no, while Haben Sie ...? presses harder.

items.hr: Das wäre sehr nett von Ihnen.
items.en: That would be very kind of you.
items.note: wäre is the softened form of ist. This sentence thanks someone before the favour has happened.

items.hr: Ich wäre Ihnen sehr dankbar.
items.en: I would be very grateful to you.
items.note: dankbar sein takes the dative: Ich bin Ihnen dankbar, ich wäre Ihnen dankbar.

items.hr: Ohne die Meldebescheinigung wäre das leider nicht möglich.
items.en: Without the registration certificate that would unfortunately not be possible.
items.note: wäre also softens a refusal, so the same no sounds much less harsh than ist nicht möglich.

activities.title: Hätte und wäre üben
activities.intro: Practise hätte and wäre in requests, thanks and refusals.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is the normal polite way to say what you want at a counter?
questions.explanation: Ich hätte gern is a fixed polite formula. Ich habe gern means you generally like something and Ich hatte gern is a past form, so neither works as a request.

questions.prompt: Which form belongs with Sie as the softened version of sein?
questions.explanation: The umlaut marks the softened form: Sie wären. waren without the umlaut is the simple past and sind is the plain present.

questions.prompt: Complete with the softened form of haben in a polite question: '___ Sie am Montag noch einen freien Termin?'
questions.explanation: Hätten Sie ...? is softer than Haben Sie ...? and leaves the other person room to answer no without embarrassment.

questions.prompt: Complete with the softened form of sein: 'Das ___ sehr nett von Ihnen.'
questions.explanation: In the third person singular the softened form of sein is wäre, and it thanks someone for a favour that has not happened yet.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I would be very grateful to you.'
questions.explanation: The verb wäre stands second, and dankbar takes the dative person Ihnen, which comes before the adverb sehr and the adjective itself.

activities.title: Ein schwieriger Wunsch
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You ask for something that is difficult to arrange and keep the whole exchange soft with hätte and wäre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, was kann ich für Sie tun?
lines.en: Good afternoon, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich hätte gern einen Termin noch in dieser Woche.
lines.en: I would like an appointment still this week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Diese Woche ist alles voll. Nächste Woche sieht es besser aus.
lines.en: This week everything is full. Next week looks better.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hätten Sie vielleicht doch noch etwas am Freitag?
lines.en: Would you perhaps still have something on Friday after all?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Einen Moment, ich schaue nach. Am Freitag um acht Uhr wäre etwas frei.
lines.en: One moment, I will check. On Friday at eight o'clock something would be free.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das wäre sehr nett von Ihnen. Ich nehme den Termin.
lines.en: That would be very kind of you. I will take the appointment.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ohne Ihre Meldebescheinigung wäre das allerdings nicht möglich.
lines.en: Without your registration certificate it would, however, not be possible.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Die bringe ich mit. Ich wäre Ihnen wirklich sehr dankbar.
lines.en: I will bring it with me. I would be really very grateful to you.

days.title: Beim Arzt, die Symptome beschreiben
days.objective: You can tell a doctor what is wrong with you, using the dative constructions that German really uses for symptoms.
days.paretoFocus: Symptoms are the one thing you cannot paraphrase your way around, and the German patterns are built on the dative, so the English word order produces sentences that mean something else.
drills: Say aloud five symptoms you have had this year, each one with a noun ending in -schmerzen.
Point at eight parts of your body and say Mein or Meine plus the part plus tut weh, with the correct article.
Take three English sentences that begin with I feel and rebuild them with the dative pronoun mir.

items: How do you say that you feel sick without making ich the subject?
Which case does wehtun take for the person who is in pain?
Name four compound nouns that end in -schmerzen.

activities.title: Was fehlt Ihnen?
activities.intro: German puts the person into the dative when it talks about how you feel: mir ist schlecht, not ich bin schlecht, which would mean that you are a bad person. The body part itself is the subject of wehtun, so it is der Hals that hurts, and the person who suffers stands in the dative. Pain as a noun is almost always a compound ending in -schmerzen, and it is always plural.

items.hr: Mir ist schlecht, und mir ist auch schwindlig.
items.en: I feel sick, and I also feel dizzy.
items.note: The person goes in the dative: mir, dir, ihm. Never ich bin schlecht.

items.hr: Mein Hals tut seit drei Tagen weh.
items.en: My throat has been hurting for three days.
items.note: der Hals. wehtun is separable, so weh goes to the end.

items.hr: Ich habe Halsschmerzen und Kopfschmerzen.
items.en: I have a sore throat and a headache.
items.note: die Schmerzen is plural: die Bauchschmerzen, die Rückenschmerzen, die Ohrenschmerzen.

items.hr: Ich fühle mich seit gestern sehr schlapp.
items.en: I have been feeling very run down since yesterday.
items.note: sich fühlen is reflexive: ich fühle mich, du fühlst dich.

items.hr: Es tut mir weh, wenn ich den Arm hebe.
items.en: It hurts me when I lift my arm.
items.note: wehtun takes a dative object: es tut mir weh, es tut ihm weh. der Arm.

activities.title: Die Symptome beschreiben
activities.intro: Practise the dative patterns and the pain nouns you will need in the consulting room.

questions.prompt: You feel nauseous. Which German sentence says that correctly?
questions.explanation: This pattern puts the person in the dative, so it is mir. Ich bin schlecht says that you are a bad person.

questions.prompt: Your stomach has been hurting all morning. Which noun completes 'Ich habe seit heute Morgen ...'?
questions.explanation: haben takes the accusative, and the pain noun is a compound of der Bauch and die Schmerzen, written as one capitalised plural.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct dative pronoun, speaking about yourself: 'Es tut ___ weh, wenn ich laufe.'
questions.explanation: wehtun takes a dative object, so the first person singular is the dative pronoun, not the accusative mich.

questions.prompt: Complete the reflexive verb: 'Ich ___ mich heute gar nicht gut.'
questions.explanation: sich fühlen is reflexive and regular: ich fühle mich, du fühlst dich, er fühlt sich.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'My throat has been hurting since Monday.'
questions.explanation: The body part is the subject, and wehtun is separable, so weh closes the sentence after the time phrase.

activities.title: Im Sprechzimmer
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You describe your symptoms to a doctor, using the dative patterns and the pain nouns from this lesson.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, was fehlt Ihnen denn?
lines.en: Hello, what seems to be the matter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Tag. Ich habe seit drei Tagen Halsschmerzen und Fieber.
lines.en: Hello. I have had a sore throat and a fever for three days.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fühlen Sie sich auch schlapp?
lines.en: Do you also feel run down?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, und heute Morgen war mir sehr schlecht.
lines.en: Yes, and this morning I felt very sick.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tut Ihnen der Kopf auch weh?
lines.en: Does your head hurt too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, der Kopf tut mir seit gestern Abend weh.
lines.en: Yes, my head has been hurting since yesterday evening.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut, ich sehe mir den Hals einmal an.
lines.en: Right, let me have a look at your throat.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Danke. Ist es etwas Schlimmes?
lines.en: Thank you. Is it anything serious?

days.title: Der Termin in der Praxis
days.objective: You can make an appointment at a doctor's practice and handle the reception desk, from the insurance card to the referral.
days.paretoFocus: Nothing happens at a German practice until you have got through the desk, and the same handful of nouns and polite forms come back at every desk you will ever stand at.
drills: Say three different polite ways to ask for an appointment, each one with hätte or könnte.
Name five things you take with you to a doctor's practice, each one with its article.
Role play the reception exchange twice, once asking for a morning slot and once for an afternoon slot.

items: Which noun is the card you hand in at the reception desk?
What do you ask your doctor for when you need to see a specialist?
What is the difference between die Sprechstunde and das Wartezimmer?

activities.title: Einen Termin vereinbaren
activities.intro: At a reception desk the polite Konjunktiv II you already know does most of the work: ich hätte gern is the normal opening. Around it sits a small set of fixed nouns that come up every single visit. Learn each noun with its article, because you will hear them back in the dative and accusative.

items.hr: Guten Tag, ich hätte gern einen Termin in dieser Woche.
items.en: Hello, I would like an appointment this week.
items.note: der Termin. ich hätte gern is the standard polite opening at a desk.

items.hr: Haben Sie am Donnerstagvormittag noch etwas frei?
items.en: Do you have anything free on Thursday morning?
items.note: der Donnerstagvormittag. am plus a part of the day gives the time.

items.hr: Bringen Sie bitte die Versichertenkarte mit.
items.en: Please bring your insurance card with you.
items.note: die Versichertenkarte, the card you hand in at the desk. mitbringen is separable.

items.hr: Nehmen Sie bitte im Wartezimmer Platz, die Sprechstunde beginnt um neun.
items.en: Please take a seat in the waiting room, surgery hours start at nine.
items.note: das Wartezimmer; die Sprechstunde, the hours when the doctor sees patients. Platz nehmen is fixed.

items.hr: Ich brauche eine Überweisung zu einem Facharzt.
items.en: I need a referral to a specialist.
items.note: die Überweisung, the referral note; der Facharzt. zu takes the dative.

activities.title: In der Praxis anmelden
activities.intro: Practise the desk vocabulary and the polite forms you need to book and register.

questions.prompt: At the desk you are asked for your health insurance card. Which German noun is it?
questions.explanation: It is built on der Versicherte, the insured person. The other three are a travel ticket, a menu and an entry ticket.

questions.prompt: Your family doctor is sending you on to a specialist. What does she write for you?
questions.explanation: die Überweisung is the referral to a Facharzt. The others mean an exercise, a surprise and an overnight stay.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite request: 'Guten Tag, ich ___ gern einen Termin.'
questions.explanation: The Konjunktiv II of haben softens the request; ich will einen Termin sounds blunt at a reception desk.

questions.prompt: Complete the separable verb: 'Bitte ___ Sie Ihre Versichertenkarte mit.'
questions.explanation: mitbringen splits in the imperative: the stem stands first and the prefix mit goes to the end.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Do you have anything free on Thursday morning?'
questions.explanation: A yes or no question puts the conjugated verb first, and the adjective frei closes the sentence after the time phrase.

activities.title: Am Empfang
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You book an appointment at the reception desk of a practice, staying with Sie throughout.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, was kann ich für Sie tun?
lines.en: Hello, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Tag, ich hätte gern einen Termin bei der Hausärztin.
lines.en: Hello, I would like an appointment with the family doctor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Waren Sie schon einmal bei uns?
lines.en: Have you been here before?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, im letzten Jahr. Ich habe die Versichertenkarte dabei.
lines.en: Yes, last year. I have my insurance card with me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut. Am Mittwoch um zehn Uhr wäre noch etwas frei.
lines.en: Good. There would still be something free on Wednesday at ten.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das passt mir gut. Wann beginnt die Sprechstunde?
lines.en: That suits me well. When do surgery hours start?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Um acht Uhr. Kommen Sie bitte zehn Minuten früher.
lines.en: At eight. Please come ten minutes early.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alles klar, vielen Dank.
lines.en: All right, thank you very much.

days.title: In der Apotheke
days.objective: You can hand in a prescription, ask for something against a symptom and understand the dosage you are given.
days.paretoFocus: A pharmacy visit is a short, predictable exchange, and getting the dosage right is the difference between a medicine working and not working.
drills: Say what you would ask for at a pharmacy counter for five different symptoms, each one with gegen.
Read the dosage line on any packet in your home and say it aloud in German with täglich.
Sort six medicines you know into rezeptfrei and rezeptpflichtig and say each one in a full sentence.

items: Which verb do you use for handing in a prescription?
Which preposition and which case follow etwas when you name the symptom?
How do you say twice a day and three times a day?

activities.title: In der Apotheke
activities.intro: A pharmacy exchange runs on three things: the prescription you hand in, the symptom you name with gegen plus the accusative, and the dosage you are told. German marks clearly whether a medicine needs a prescription or not, with the adjectives rezeptpflichtig and rezeptfrei. The dosage is normally a number plus -mal followed by täglich.

items.hr: Ich möchte dieses Rezept einlösen.
items.en: I would like to hand in this prescription.
items.note: das Rezept; ein Rezept einlösen is the fixed verb for having it filled.

items.hr: Haben Sie etwas gegen Husten?
items.en: Do you have something for a cough?
items.note: der Husten. gegen takes the accusative: gegen Fieber, gegen Kopfschmerzen.

items.hr: Ist das Medikament rezeptpflichtig?
items.en: Does that medicine need a prescription?
items.note: das Medikament. The suffix -pflichtig means required, and -frei means not required.

items.hr: Nehmen Sie dreimal täglich eine Tablette nach dem Essen.
items.en: Take one tablet three times a day after meals.
items.note: die Tablette. nach takes the dative, so das Essen becomes dem Essen.

items.hr: Die Dosierung finden Sie auf dem Beipackzettel.
items.en: You will find the dosage on the information leaflet.
items.note: die Dosierung; der Beipackzettel, the leaflet inside the box.

activities.title: Medikamente kaufen
activities.intro: Practise handing in a prescription, naming a symptom and reading a dosage.

questions.prompt: You have a prescription and want it filled. What do you say at the counter?
