questions.explanation: einlösen is the verb for redeeming a prescription or a voucher. das Rezept also means a cooking recipe, which is why the other options sound absurd here.

questions.prompt: Which adjective describes a medicine you can only get with a prescription?
questions.explanation: The suffix -pflichtig comes from die Pflicht, the duty, so a prescription is obligatory. Its opposite is rezeptfrei.

questions.prompt: Complete the question at the counter: 'Haben Sie etwas ___ Halsschmerzen?'
questions.explanation: This preposition names the problem the medicine works against, and it takes the accusative.

questions.prompt: Complete the dosage: 'Nehmen Sie ___ täglich eine Tablette, morgens, mittags und abends.'
questions.explanation: A number plus -mal gives the frequency, written as one word: einmal, zweimal, dreimal.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'You will find the dosage on the information leaflet.'
questions.explanation: The object stands first for emphasis, so the verb keeps position two. auf answers wo here and therefore takes the dative.

activities.title: Am Schalter der Apotheke
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You hand in a prescription and buy something for a cough, using gegen and the dosage forms.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, was darf es sein?
lines.en: Hello, what can I get you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Tag, ich möchte dieses Rezept einlösen.
lines.en: Hello, I would like to hand in this prescription.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gern. Brauchen Sie sonst noch etwas?
lines.en: Gladly. Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, haben Sie etwas gegen Husten?
lines.en: Yes, do you have something for a cough?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dieser Hustensaft hilft gut und ist rezeptfrei.
lines.en: This cough syrup works well and needs no prescription.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Wie oft soll ich ihn nehmen?
lines.en: How often should I take it?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dreimal täglich einen Löffel, am besten nach dem Essen.
lines.en: One spoonful three times a day, ideally after meals.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vielen Dank, dann nehme ich ihn.
lines.en: Thank you very much, I will take it then.

days.title: Die Krankmeldung beim Arbeitgeber
days.objective: You can call your employer to report that you are ill and say what you know about the certificate and the deadline.
days.paretoFocus: This call has to be produced under stress, on the worst morning of your month, so it has to be a script you can say without thinking.
drills: Say your sick call script aloud from memory in under twenty seconds.
Write down from memory when you must inform an employer and where the number of days is fixed.
Turn three sentences into the perfect with krankschreiben.

items: On which day of your absence do you inform your employer?
Where is the number of days before a certificate is required written down?
How do you say that you have to call in sick, using a reflexive verb?

activities.title: Sich krankmelden
activities.intro: In Germany you tell your employer that you are ill as early as possible on the first day of your absence, normally by phone before your working day would start. From a set number of days of absence a doctor's certificate is required, and the exact number is fixed in your own employment contract, so it is worth checking that before you ever need it. The verbs here are reflexive and separable, which is why they need practice as whole sentences.

items.hr: Ich muss mich heute krankmelden.
items.en: I have to call in sick today.
items.note: sich krankmelden is reflexive and separable: ich melde mich krank.

items.hr: Ich bin leider krank und kann heute nicht zur Arbeit kommen.
items.en: I am ill, unfortunately, and cannot come to work today.
items.note: die Arbeit. zu plus der dative die Arbeit contracts to zur.

items.hr: Ich gehe am Vormittag zum Arzt und melde mich danach wieder.
items.en: I am going to the doctor this morning and will get back to you afterwards.
items.note: der Arzt. zu plus dem Arzt contracts to zum.

items.hr: Ab wie vielen Tagen brauchen Sie eine Krankmeldung?
items.en: From how many days of absence do you need a certificate?
items.note: die Krankmeldung, the certificate of sick leave. The number stands in your Arbeitsvertrag.

items.hr: Die Ärztin hat mich für drei Tage krankgeschrieben.
items.en: The doctor has signed me off sick for three days.
items.note: krankschreiben, to sign someone off sick. Perfect: hat krankgeschrieben.

activities.title: Der Anruf im Betrieb
activities.intro: Practise the procedure and the exact wording of the call.

questions.prompt: When do you have to tell your employer in Germany that you are ill?
options: As early as possible on the first day of your absence
Only once you have seen a doctor
At the end of the week
Only if you will be away for more than a week
questions.answer: As early as possible on the first day of your absence
questions.explanation: The employer has to know without delay, normally by phone before your working day would start. The doctor's certificate is a separate, later step.

questions.prompt: Who fixes from how many days of absence you must hand in a doctor's certificate?
options: The reception desk of the practice
questions.explanation: A certificate is required from a set number of days, and the exact number is written in the individual employment contract, so it differs from job to job.

questions.prompt: Complete the reflexive verb: 'Ich muss ___ heute leider krankmelden.'
questions.explanation: sich krankmelden is reflexive, so the first person singular needs the accusative pronoun.

questions.prompt: Complete the contracted form: 'Ich kann heute nicht ___ Arbeit kommen.'
questions.explanation: zu always takes the dative, and with the feminine die Arbeit the two words contract into one.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'The doctor has signed me off sick for three days.'
questions.explanation: The perfect puts hat in position two and the participle at the very end, with the prefix krank in front of geschrieben.

activities.title: Der Anruf am Morgen
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You phone your employer on the first morning of your illness and ask about the certificate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Morgen, was ist denn los?
lines.en: Good morning, what is the matter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Morgen. Ich muss mich leider krankmelden, ich habe seit heute Nacht hohes Fieber.
lines.en: Good morning. I have to call in sick, unfortunately, I have had a high fever since last night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Das tut mir leid. Bleiben Sie zu Hause.
lines.en: I am sorry to hear that. Stay at home.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Danke. Ab wie vielen Tagen brauchen Sie eine Krankmeldung?
lines.en: Thank you. From how many days do you need a certificate?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Das steht in Ihrem Arbeitsvertrag, schauen Sie bitte heute noch nach.
lines.en: That is in your employment contract, please check it today.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mache ich. Ich gehe am Vormittag zum Arzt und melde mich danach wieder.
lines.en: I will. I am going to the doctor this morning and will get back to you afterwards.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut. Gute Besserung!
lines.en: Good. Get well soon!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vielen Dank, auf Wiederhören.
lines.en: Thank you very much, goodbye.

days.title: Der Notfall und der Notruf
days.objective: You can make an emergency call in German and give the information in the order the emergency service needs it.
days.paretoFocus: This is the one conversation where a missing word costs time that matters, and the structure is fixed, so it can be learned once and kept for life.
drills: Say the four pieces of information of an emergency call in the correct order, out loud, twice.
Give your own address in German exactly as you would give it on an emergency call.
Say three short sentences about what has happened, each one in the perfect with sein.

items: Which number reaches the fire service and the ambulance?
Which piece of information comes first on the call, and why?
When may you end an emergency call?

activities.title: Der Notruf 112
activities.intro: In Germany and across Europe you reach the fire service and the ambulance on 112, free of charge from any phone. Give the information in a fixed order: where it happened, what happened, how many people are hurt, and then wait for the questions of the person on the line. Never hang up first, because they may still need details or may talk you through first aid.

items.hr: Der Notruf 112 erreicht die Feuerwehr und den Rettungsdienst.
items.en: The emergency number 112 reaches the fire service and the ambulance service.
items.note: der Notruf; die Feuerwehr; der Rettungsdienst. In Germany the police answer on 110.

items.hr: Der Unfall ist in der Gartenstraße 8, im zweiten Stock.
items.en: The accident is at Gartenstrasse 8, on the second floor.
items.note: Say the place first: die Straße, die Hausnummer, der Stock.

items.hr: Ein Mann ist gestürzt und blutet stark.
items.en: A man has fallen and is bleeding heavily.
items.note: stürzen and passieren take sein in the perfect: ist gestürzt, ist passiert.

items.hr: Es gibt zwei Verletzte, eine Person ist bewusstlos.
items.en: There are two injured people, one person is unconscious.
items.note: der Verletzte, die Verletzten, an adjectival noun. bewusstlos, unconscious.

items.hr: Bitte schicken Sie schnell einen Krankenwagen.
items.en: Please send an ambulance quickly.
items.note: der Krankenwagen. Then stay on the line and wait for their questions.

activities.title: Den Notruf absetzen
activities.intro: Practise the emergency number, the order of the information and the perfect forms you need.

questions.prompt: Which number reaches the fire service and the ambulance in Germany and across Europe?
questions.explanation: 112 is the European emergency number and is free from any phone. In Germany 110 reaches the police.

questions.prompt: What do you say first once the emergency call is answered?
options: The name of your family doctor
questions.explanation: The place comes first so that help can be sent even if the call breaks off. Then what happened, then how many people are hurt.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Bei dem Unfall gibt es zwei ___, eine Person ist bewusstlos.'
questions.explanation: This is an adjectival noun. After the bare number zwei it takes the plural ending -e and, like every German noun, keeps its capital letter.

questions.prompt: Complete the perfect of stürzen: 'Eine Frau ist auf der nassen Treppe ___.'
questions.explanation: stürzen describes a change of position, so it forms the perfect with sein, and as a regular verb its participle ends in -t.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Please send an ambulance quickly.'
questions.explanation: In the Sie imperative the verb comes first and Sie follows it directly; der Krankenwagen is the accusative object and becomes einen Krankenwagen.

activities.title: Am Telefon mit der Leitstelle
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You call 112 for a neighbour and stay calm while giving each detail the operator asks for.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Notruf 112, wo ist der Notfall?
lines.en: Emergency call 112, where is the emergency?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: In der Gartenstraße 8, im dritten Stock.
lines.en: At Gartenstrasse 8, on the third floor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Was ist passiert?
lines.en: What has happened?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Meine Nachbarin ist gestürzt und kann nicht mehr aufstehen.
lines.en: My neighbour has fallen and cannot get up any more.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wie viele Personen sind verletzt?
lines.en: How many people are injured?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nur eine Person. Sie ist bei Bewusstsein, aber ihr Bein tut sehr weh.
lines.en: Only one person. She is conscious, but her leg hurts a lot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut. Bleiben Sie bei ihr, der Krankenwagen kommt sofort.
lines.en: Good. Stay with her, the ambulance is on its way.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich bleibe hier und warte auf Ihre Fragen.
lines.en: I will stay here and wait for your questions.

days.title: Einen Rat geben mit sollte
days.objective: You can give advice with sollte and keep it clearly apart from an obligation expressed with müssen.
days.paretoFocus: Advice and orders sound the same in English but not in German, and using müssen where a native speaker would use sollte makes a friendly suggestion sound like a command.
drills: Give five pieces of advice to a friend with a cold, all of them with solltest.
Turn three of those sentences into obligations with musst and say aloud how the meaning changes.
Ask three careful questions that begin with Solltest du.

items: What are the six forms of the advice modal?
What is the difference in meaning between du solltest and du musst?
Which word softens a piece of advice that you give to yourself?

activities.title: Ratschläge geben mit sollte
activities.intro: sollte is the polite advice form of sollen: du solltest mehr schlafen recommends something and leaves the choice with the listener, while du musst mehr schlafen states an obligation. The forms are ich sollte, du solltest, er sollte, wir sollten, ihr solltet and Sie sollten, and like every modal it sends the infinitive to the end of the sentence.

items.hr: Du solltest mehr schlafen, du siehst müde aus.
items.en: You should sleep more, you look tired.
items.note: sollte recommends; the listener can still decide otherwise. aussehen is separable.

items.hr: Du musst heute noch das Formular unterschreiben.
items.en: You have to sign the form today.
items.note: das Formular. müssen is a real obligation, not a recommendation.

items.hr: Sie sollten weniger Zucker essen und mehr Wasser trinken.
items.en: You should eat less sugar and drink more water.
items.note: der Zucker; das Wasser. The Sie form is the same as the wir form.

items.hr: Ich sollte eigentlich früher ins Bett gehen.
items.en: I really ought to go to bed earlier.
items.note: eigentlich softens it: you know it and you are not doing it.

items.hr: Solltest du nicht lieber zum Arzt gehen?
items.en: Should you not rather go to the doctor?
items.note: As a question the form sounds careful and friendly. lieber adds a preference.

activities.title: Rat oder Pflicht
activities.intro: Practise the forms of the advice modal and the contrast with a real obligation.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is friendly advice rather than an obligation?
questions.explanation: sollte recommends and leaves the choice open. müssen states an obligation, dürfen gives permission and wollen states a wish.

questions.prompt: Which form fits the du address here: '___ du nicht lieber zu Hause bleiben?'
questions.explanation: The advice form for du ends in -est, and it stands in first position because the sentence is a yes or no question.

questions.prompt: Complete the advice to somebody you address formally: 'Sie ___ weniger Zucker essen.'
questions.explanation: The formal address takes the same ending -en as wir and as the plural sie.

questions.prompt: Complete with the modal of real obligation: 'Ich ___ morgen um sechs Uhr aufstehen, sonst verpasse ich den Zug.'
questions.explanation: The train will not wait, so this is an obligation and not a recommendation, and the advice form would wrongly leave you the choice.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'You should drink more water.'
questions.explanation: The modal takes position two and the infinitive trinken closes the sentence.

activities.title: Ein guter Rat
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A doctor gives you advice with sollte and you check what is advice and what is an obligation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ihre Werte sind in Ordnung, aber Sie sind oft müde, richtig?
lines.en: Your results are fine, but you are often tired, is that right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, ich fühle mich schon seit Wochen schlapp.
lines.en: Yes, I have been feeling run down for weeks.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dann sollten Sie abends früher ins Bett gehen.
lines.en: Then you should go to bed earlier in the evening.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Und was sollte ich beim Essen ändern?
lines.en: And what should I change about my eating?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sie sollten weniger Zucker essen und mehr Wasser trinken.
lines.en: You should eat less sugar and drink more water.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muss ich ganz auf Kaffee verzichten?
lines.en: Do I have to give up coffee completely?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nein, das müssen Sie nicht. Zwei Tassen am Tag sind kein Problem.
lines.en: No, you do not have to. Two cups a day are no problem.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut, dann probiere ich Ihren Rat vier Wochen lang aus.
lines.en: Good, then I will try your advice for four weeks.

days.title: Eine Stellenanzeige lesen
days.objective: You can read a German job advert and pick out the working hours, the contract type and the documents you are asked to send.
days.paretoFocus: Job adverts repeat the same fifteen or twenty words, so learning that small set once unlocks every advert you will ever read.
drills: Write out the four blocks a typical advert has and one German phrase you expect in each.
Say aloud the difference between Vollzeit and Teilzeit, and between befristet and unbefristet.
List the three documents that make up the Bewerbungsunterlagen, each with its article.

items: Which article does a compound noun take, and why is it die Berufserfahrung?
What is the difference between erwünscht and erforderlich in an advert?
Should you apply if you meet most but not all of the listed requirements? Why?

activities.title: Wörter in der Stellenanzeige
activities.intro: German job adverts are built from a fixed set of blocks: who is wanted, what the tasks are, what the employer expects, and what the employer offers. Most of the vocabulary comes from compound nouns, and a compound noun always takes the gender of its last part. One honest note: German adverts routinely list far more requirements than are strictly necessary, so a good match on most points is usually enough to apply.

items.hr: Wir suchen eine Mitarbeiterin oder einen Mitarbeiter in Vollzeit.
items.en: We are looking for an employee (female) or an employee (male) full time.
items.note: die Vollzeit is roughly 40 Stunden pro Woche, die Teilzeit is anything clearly below that.

items.hr: Die Stelle ist zunächst befristet, danach ist ein unbefristeter Vertrag möglich.
items.en: The position is limited in time at first, after that a permanent contract is possible.
items.note: befristet = fixed term with an end date, unbefristet = permanent, with no end date.

items.hr: Sie haben mindestens zwei Jahre Berufserfahrung.
items.en: You have at least two years of professional experience.
items.note: die Berufserfahrung comes from der Beruf plus die Erfahrung, so the compound is feminine.

items.hr: Gute Kenntnisse in der deutschen Sprache sind erwünscht.
items.en: Good knowledge of the German language is desired.
items.note: erwünscht is a wish, erforderlich means genuinely required. die Kenntnisse is used in the plural.

items.hr: Bitte schicken Sie uns Ihre vollständigen Bewerbungsunterlagen.
items.en: Please send us your complete application documents.
items.note: die Bewerbungsunterlagen, plural: das Anschreiben, der Lebenslauf und die Zeugnisse.

activities.title: Die Anzeige verstehen
activities.intro: Practise the standard vocabulary of job adverts and the gender of compound nouns.

questions.prompt: An advert says: 'Die Stelle ist auf ein Jahr befristet.' What does that mean?
options: The position is limited to one year.
The position starts in one year.
questions.answer: The position is limited to one year.
questions.explanation: befristet means the contract has a fixed end date. The opposite, unbefristet, has no end date.

questions.prompt: Welcher Artikel passt zu Berufserfahrung?
questions.explanation: A compound noun takes the gender of its last part. The last part is die Erfahrung, so it is die Berufserfahrung.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Wir suchen eine Kollegin in ____, also für etwa vierzig Stunden pro Woche.'
questions.explanation: Around forty hours a week is die Vollzeit. Fewer hours would be die Teilzeit. German nouns keep their capital letter here too.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Diese Stelle ist nicht auf zwei Jahre begrenzt, sondern ____.'
questions.explanation: A contract with no end date is unbefristet. Used here as a predicate adjective, so it takes no ending.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Please send us your complete application documents.'
questions.explanation: In a polite imperative with Sie the verb comes first and Sie follows it. The dative object uns comes before the accusative object.

activities.title: Wir lesen eine Anzeige
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An acquaintance reads a job advert out loud and you explain the key words to her.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hier steht: Wir suchen eine Mitarbeiterin in Teilzeit, zwanzig Stunden pro Woche. Ist das viel?
lines.en: It says here: We are looking for an employee part time, twenty hours a week. Is that a lot?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nein, das ist die Hälfte. Vollzeit sind ungefähr vierzig Stunden.
lines.en: No, that is half. Full time is about forty hours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und die Stelle ist auf ein Jahr befristet. Muss ich dann nach einem Jahr gehen?
lines.en: And the position is limited to one year. Do I have to leave after a year then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nicht unbedingt. Viele Betriebe machen danach einen unbefristeten Vertrag.
lines.en: Not necessarily. Many companies offer a permanent contract after that.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sie wollen drei Jahre Berufserfahrung. Ich habe nur zwei.
lines.en: They want three years of professional experience. I only have two.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bewerben Sie sich trotzdem. In Anzeigen steht oft mehr, als wirklich nötig ist.
lines.en: Apply anyway. Adverts often list more than is really necessary.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut. Und was soll ich schicken?
lines.en: All right. And what should I send?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Die Bewerbungsunterlagen: das Anschreiben, den Lebenslauf und Ihre Zeugnisse.
lines.en: The application documents: the cover letter, the CV and your references.

days.title: Der Lebenslauf
days.objective: You can write a German CV in the expected tabular form and describe your jobs and training with von, bis and seit.
days.paretoFocus: The German CV follows one rigid layout, and getting that layout right matters more than the words inside it.
drills: Write the five section headings of a tabular CV in German, each with its article.
Write three lines about your own past jobs using von ... bis ... and the perfect tense.
Write one line about your current situation using seit plus the present tense.

items: In which order do the jobs appear, and where does the newest one go?
Which two personal details are no longer expected, and is a photo required?
What are the last three things at the bottom of the page?

activities.title: Der tabellarische Lebenslauf
activities.intro: A German CV is tabellarisch, meaning a two column table of dates and entries, never continuous prose. It runs in reverse chronological order and usually has the sections Persönliche Daten, Berufserfahrung, Ausbildung, Kenntnisse und Sprachen. Older guides ask for a photo, a date of birth and a marital status; none of these is expected today.

items.hr: Der Lebenslauf ist tabellarisch und beginnt mit der neuesten Stelle.
items.en: The CV is tabular and starts with the most recent position.
items.note: Reverse chronological order: newest at the top, oldest at the bottom.

items.hr: Von 2019 bis 2023 habe ich als Verkäuferin in einem Supermarkt gearbeitet.
items.en: From 2019 to 2023 I worked as a salesperson in a supermarket.
items.note: von ... bis ... marks a finished period. Years take no preposition of their own and no article.

items.hr: Seit März 2024 arbeite ich als Bürokaufmann in Teilzeit.
items.en: Since March 2024 I have been working as an office clerk part time.
items.note: seit plus Dativ plus the present tense for something that is still going on. German uses the present here, not the perfect.

items.hr: Unten stehen der Ort, das Datum und meine Unterschrift.
items.en: At the bottom are the place, the date and my signature.
items.note: The German CV is dated and signed by hand, unlike in many other countries.

items.hr: Ein Foto ist heute freiwillig, und der Familienstand gehört nicht mehr in den Lebenslauf.
items.en: A photo is optional today, and marital status no longer belongs in the CV.
items.note: Das Geburtsdatum ist ebenfalls freiwillig. Older templates still have fields for all of these.

activities.title: Angaben im Lebenslauf
activities.intro: Practise the layout of the German CV and the time expressions used in it.

questions.prompt: In a German Lebenslauf, in which order do you list your jobs?
questions.explanation: The CV is reverse chronological, so the reader sees your current or most recent position immediately.

questions.prompt: Welches Wort passt? '____ 2021 wohne ich in Hamburg.'
questions.explanation: The situation started in 2021 and is still true, so German uses seit plus the present tense.

questions.prompt: Complete this finished period: '____ 2018 bis 2022 habe ich eine Ausbildung gemacht.'
questions.explanation: A closed period with a start and an end takes von ... bis ... The perfect tense shows it is over.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ganz unten auf dem Blatt stehen der Ort, das Datum und meine ____.'
questions.explanation: die Unterschrift, from unterschreiben. A German CV is signed by hand at the bottom.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Since March I have been working part time.'
questions.explanation: The time expression comes first, so the verb stays in second position and the subject moves behind it.

activities.title: Wir prüfen den Lebenslauf
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone shows you a draft CV and you correct it against the German expectations.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich habe meinen Lebenslauf geschrieben. Brauche ich ein Foto?
lines.en: I have written my CV. Do I need a photo?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nein, ein Foto ist freiwillig. Viele Bewerberinnen und Bewerber schicken heute keins mehr.
lines.en: No, a photo is optional. Many applicants no longer send one today.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und mein Geburtsdatum? Meine alte Vorlage hat ein Feld dafür.
lines.en: And my date of birth? My old template has a field for it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das müssen Sie nicht angeben, den Familienstand auch nicht.
lines.en: You do not have to state that, nor your marital status.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meine Stellen habe ich sortiert, die älteste steht ganz oben.
lines.en: I have sorted my positions, the oldest one is right at the top.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Drehen Sie das bitte um. Die neueste Stelle gehört nach oben.
lines.en: Please turn that around. The most recent position belongs at the top.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut. Fehlt sonst noch etwas?
lines.en: Fine. Is anything else missing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, unten der Ort mit dem Datum und darunter Ihre Unterschrift.
lines.en: Yes, at the bottom the place with the date and your signature below it.

days.title: Das Bewerbungsschreiben
days.objective: You can write a one page German cover letter with the correct opening, closing and the fixed verb plus preposition combinations it needs.
days.paretoFocus: Four fixed formulas carry most of a German cover letter, and using them correctly signals that you know the conventions.
drills: Write the opening line for an advert with no contact name and for one naming a woman.
Write one sentence naming the exact role you are applying for.
Write the closing sentence about an invitation and the sign off underneath it.

items: Which preposition follows sich bewerben for the job, and which one for the company?
Why must the specific role appear in the first line?
Is there a comma after the sign off?

activities.title: Das Anschreiben formulieren
activities.intro: The German cover letter fits on one page and stays formal with Sie throughout. Its power comes from a handful of set phrases, plus verbs that always keep the same preposition. Naming the exact role in the first line matters because a reader handling several open positions must know at once which advert you mean.

items.hr: Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren,
items.en: Dear Sir or Madam,
items.note: Use this when the advert gives no contact name. With a name: Sehr geehrte Frau Berger, or Sehr geehrter Herr Berger,

items.hr: Ich bewerbe mich um die Stelle als Bürokauffrau in Ihrem Betrieb.
items.en: I am applying for the position of office clerk in your company.
items.note: sich bewerben um plus Akkusativ for the job, sich bewerben bei plus Dativ for the employer.

items.hr: Ich interessiere mich sehr für diese Aufgabe, weil ich gern im Team arbeite.
items.en: I am very interested in this job, because I like working in a team.
items.note: sich interessieren für plus Akkusativ. The preposition never changes.

items.hr: Über eine Einladung zu einem Vorstellungsgespräch freue ich mich sehr.
items.en: I would be very glad to receive an invitation to an interview.
items.note: sich freuen über plus Akkusativ. This is the standard last sentence of the letter.

items.hr: Mit freundlichen Grüßen
items.en: Yours sincerely
items.note: No comma after the sign off. Your name goes on the next line. Note the ß after the long vowel in Grüßen.

activities.title: Formeln im Anschreiben
activities.intro: Practise the fixed openings, closings and prepositions of a German cover letter.

questions.prompt: The advert gives no contact name. How do you open the letter?
questions.explanation: This is the neutral formal opening when you do not know who will read the letter. The other three are far too informal.

questions.prompt: Welche Präposition passt? 'Ich interessiere mich ____ diese Aufgabe.'
questions.explanation: sich interessieren always takes für plus Akkusativ, whatever the object is.

questions.prompt: Complete the sign off at the end of a formal letter: 'Mit freundlichen ____'
questions.explanation: der Gruß becomes die Grüße in the plural, and after mit it takes the Dativ ending: Grüßen. The ß follows a long vowel.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich ____ mich sehr über eine Einladung zum Vorstellungsgespräch.'
questions.explanation: sich freuen über plus Akkusativ. With ich the verb ends in e and the reflexive pronoun is mich.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I am applying for the position of salesperson.'
questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun follows the conjugated verb, then the fixed preposition um with the Akkusativ. After als the job title takes no article.

activities.title: Der erste Satz im Anschreiben
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An adviser reads your draft cover letter and you rewrite the weak parts using the set formulas.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ihr Anschreiben beginnt mit: Ich suche eine neue Arbeit. Das ist zu allgemein.
lines.en: Your cover letter starts with: I am looking for a new job. That is too general.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Was soll ich stattdessen schreiben?
lines.en: What should I write instead?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nennen Sie im ersten Satz die Stelle. Sonst weiß niemand, welche Anzeige Sie meinen.
lines.en: Name the position in the first sentence. Otherwise nobody knows which advert you mean.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Also: Ich bewerbe mich um die Stelle als Bürokauffrau in Ihrem Betrieb.
lines.en: So: I am applying for the position of office clerk in your company.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Genau so. Und wie schließen Sie den Brief?
lines.en: Exactly like that. And how do you close the letter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mit freundlichen Grüßen, und darunter mein Name.
lines.en: Yours sincerely, and my name below it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Richtig. Und die Anrede oben?
lines.en: Correct. And the salutation at the top?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: In der Anzeige steht kein Name, also schreibe ich: Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren.
lines.en: The advert gives no name, so I write: Dear Sir or Madam.

days.title: Das Vorstellungsgespräch
days.objective: You can answer the four questions that come up in almost every German interview and ask one sensible question back.
days.paretoFocus: The same four questions appear in nearly every interview, so a prepared shape for each removes most of the pressure.
drills: Prepare a three step answer about your own background: past, training, present.
Write one real strength and one real weakness, each with what you do about it.
Write two questions you could ask at the end of an interview.

items: Which reflexive pronoun follows a preposition when the polite Sie is used?
What makes a weakness answer credible rather than empty?
Why is saying no to the final question a bad idea?

activities.title: Fragen im Vorstellungsgespräch
activities.intro: Four questions dominate German interviews, and each has a shape that works. Note also what happens to the reflexive pronoun after a preposition: with ich it is mich, with the polite Sie it is sich. Keep to Sie for the whole interview, however friendly the atmosphere becomes.

items.hr: Erzählen Sie uns bitte etwas über sich.
items.en: Please tell us something about yourself.
items.note: After a preposition the reflexive with Sie is sich. Your own answer starts: Ich erzähle Ihnen etwas über mich.

items.hr: Ich habe drei Jahre im Verkauf gearbeitet und danach eine Weiterbildung gemacht.
items.en: I worked in sales for three years and after that I did some further training.
items.note: A good shape for that question: two or three steps in the past, then where you are now. Sixty seconds is enough.

items.hr: Ich bewerbe mich bei Ihnen, weil mich die Arbeit mit Kundinnen und Kunden interessiert.
items.en: I am applying to you because working with customers interests me.
items.note: Answer Warum bewerben Sie sich bei uns with something concrete from the advert, never with Weil ich Geld brauche.

items.hr: Meine Stärke ist, dass ich ruhig bleibe. Früher habe ich Termine vergessen, deshalb arbeite ich jetzt mit einem Kalender.
items.en: My strength is that I stay calm. I used to forget appointments, so now I work with a calendar.
items.note: die Stärke, die Schwäche. Name a real weakness and add what you did about it.

items.hr: Haben Sie noch Fragen an uns?
items.en: Do you have any questions for us?
items.note: Always have one ready, for example: Wie sieht der erste Monat aus? Saying nein looks uninterested.

activities.title: Antworten vorbereiten
activities.intro: Practise the standard interview questions and the reflexive forms they use.

questions.prompt: Welches Reflexivpronomen passt? 'Erzählen Sie etwas über ____.'
questions.explanation: The subject is the polite Sie, so the reflexive form after the preposition is sich, not Sie.

questions.prompt: You are asked about a weakness. Which answer works best?
options: Ich habe Termine vergessen, deshalb nutze ich jetzt einen Kalender.
questions.answer: Ich habe Termine vergessen, deshalb nutze ich jetzt einen Kalender.
questions.explanation: A concrete weakness plus the solution shows self awareness. Denying any weakness or refusing to answer sounds evasive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Meine ____ ist, dass ich auch bei viel Stress ruhig bleibe.'
questions.explanation: die Stärke is a strong point, die Schwäche its opposite. Both are feminine nouns and keep their capital letter.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich ____ mich bei Ihnen, weil mich der Kontakt mit Menschen interessiert.'
questions.explanation: sich bewerben bei plus Dativ for the employer. With ich the verb ends in e and takes mich.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Please tell us something about yourself.'
questions.explanation: Polite imperative: verb first, then Sie. The dative uns comes before etwas, and the preposition über takes the reflexive sich.

activities.title: Im Vorstellungsgespräch
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are the candidate and answer the four standard questions with a clear shape each time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, schön, dass Sie da sind. Erzählen Sie uns bitte etwas über sich.
lines.en: Hello, good that you are here. Please tell us something about yourself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gern. Ich habe drei Jahre im Verkauf gearbeitet und danach eine Weiterbildung im Büro gemacht.
lines.en: Gladly. I worked in sales for three years and after that I did further training in office work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und warum bewerben Sie sich bei uns?
lines.en: And why are you applying to us?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Weil mich der Kontakt mit Kundinnen und Kunden interessiert. Genau das stand in Ihrer Anzeige.
lines.en: Because contact with customers interests me. That is exactly what your advert said.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Was ist Ihre größte Schwäche?
lines.en: What is your greatest weakness?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich habe früher Termine vergessen. Deshalb arbeite ich jetzt konsequent mit einem Kalender.
lines.en: I used to forget appointments. That is why I now work consistently with a calendar.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Haben Sie noch Fragen an uns?
lines.en: Do you have any questions for us?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja. Wie sieht der erste Monat für neue Kolleginnen und Kollegen aus?
lines.en: Yes. What does the first month look like for new colleagues?

days.title: Der Arbeitsvertrag und die Arbeitszeit
days.objective: You can read the key clauses of a German employment contract and ask about probation, notice, holiday, pay and overtime.
days.paretoFocus: Five clauses decide what your working life actually looks like, and each one has a fixed German word you will meet again and again.
drills: Write the five contract words with their articles and a one line English gloss for each.
Say aloud the difference between brutto and netto in your own words.
Write two questions you would ask before signing a contract.

items: How long is the probation period usually, and what changes about the notice period during it?
How does the statutory minimum holiday compare with what most contracts actually offer?
Which verb is used for stating amounts and periods in a contract?

activities.title: Der Arbeitsvertrag
activities.intro: A German employment contract is built around a small number of clauses that always carry the same names. The verb betragen is the standard way to state an amount or a period, and dauern is used for how long something lasts. Watch the gap between the legal minimum for holiday and what contracts really give, because the two are far apart.

items.hr: Die Probezeit dauert meistens sechs Monate.
items.en: The probation period usually lasts six months.
items.note: die Probezeit. During it the notice period is shorter, often two weeks for both sides.

items.hr: Die Kündigungsfrist beträgt vier Wochen zum Monatsende.
items.en: The notice period is four weeks to the end of the month.
items.note: betragen is used for amounts and periods, roughly like to amount to. die Kündigungsfrist.

items.hr: Der gesetzliche Mindesturlaub liegt bei zwanzig Tagen im Jahr, aber die meisten Verträge geben deutlich mehr.
items.en: The statutory minimum holiday is twenty days a year, but most contracts give considerably more.
items.note: Twenty days assumes a five day week. Typical contracts offer 25 to 30 Urlaubstage.

items.hr: Mein Gehalt beträgt brutto 2.800 Euro im Monat.
items.en: My salary is 2,800 euros gross per month.
items.note: brutto is before tax and contributions, netto is what actually arrives in your account, clearly less.

items.hr: Meine Überstunden kann ich später als freie Tage nehmen.
items.en: I can take my overtime as days off later.
items.note: die Überstunde, almost always in the plural. The alternative is Überstunden bezahlen.

activities.title: Zahlen im Vertrag
activities.intro: Practise the contract vocabulary and the way German states amounts and periods.

questions.prompt: A contract says: 'Das Gehalt beträgt brutto 2.800 Euro.' What does the person actually receive each month?
options: Clearly less, after tax and social contributions
Nothing until the probation period ends
questions.answer: Clearly less, after tax and social contributions
questions.explanation: brutto is the figure before deductions. The netto amount that reaches the account is noticeably lower.

questions.prompt: Welches Verb passt? 'Die Kündigungsfrist ____ vier Wochen.'
questions.explanation: betragen is the standard verb for stating an amount or a length in contracts and official texts.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'In den ersten sechs Monaten bin ich noch in der ____.'
questions.explanation: die Probezeit is the trial period at the start of a job, when notice periods are shorter.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Wenn ich länger arbeite als vereinbart, mache ich ____.'
questions.explanation: die Überstunden, plural. You either get them paid or take them back as time off.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'The probation period usually lasts six months.'
questions.explanation: Subject first, verb second, then the adverb meistens before the time expression.

activities.title: Fragen zum Vertrag
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your new employer hands you the contract and you ask about each clause before signing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Hier ist Ihr Arbeitsvertrag. Haben Sie Fragen dazu?
lines.en: Here is your employment contract. Do you have any questions about it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja. Wie lange dauert die Probezeit?
lines.en: Yes. How long is the probation period?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sechs Monate. In dieser Zeit beträgt die Kündigungsfrist nur zwei Wochen.
lines.en: Six months. During that time the notice period is only two weeks.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Und wie viele Urlaubstage bekomme ich im Jahr?
lines.en: And how many holiday days do I get per year?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Achtundzwanzig. Das Gesetz verlangt nur zwanzig, wir geben mehr.
lines.en: Twenty-eight. The law requires only twenty, we give more.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das Gehalt im Vertrag ist brutto, richtig?
lines.en: The salary in the contract is gross, correct?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Richtig. Und Überstunden nehmen Sie bei uns später als freie Tage.
lines.en: Correct. And you take overtime here as days off later.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut. Dann lese ich alles in Ruhe und unterschreibe morgen.
lines.en: Good. Then I will read everything calmly and sign tomorrow.

days.title: Am ersten Arbeitstag
days.objective: You can introduce yourself on your first day and understand who is showing you what, using verbs that take a person in the dative and a thing in the accusative.
days.paretoFocus: Your first day is nothing but introductions and being shown things, and both run on the same handful of verbs.
drills: Write your own two sentence self introduction for a first day.
Write three sentences with zeigen, erklären and geben, each with a dative person and an accusative thing.
Write two polite questions you would need on a first day.

items: Which case does the person take after zeigen, geben and erklären, and which case does the thing take?
How does the separable verb sich vorstellen split in a normal statement?
Which pronoun replaces mir when you are talking about someone else?

activities.title: Der erste Arbeitstag
activities.intro: On a first day you mostly introduce yourself and get shown around, so two things matter. First, sich vorstellen is reflexive and separable. Second, verbs like zeigen, geben and erklären take the person in the Dativ and the thing in the Akkusativ, and that pattern never changes.

items.hr: Darf ich mich kurz vorstellen? Ich bin die neue Kollegin.
items.en: May I briefly introduce myself? I am the new colleague.
items.note: sich vorstellen is separable: Ich stelle mich vor. In a normal statement the prefix vor goes to the end.

items.hr: Meine Chefin zeigt mir das Büro und erklärt mir die Technik.
items.en: My boss shows me the office and explains the equipment to me.
items.note: zeigen und erklären: die Person im Dativ (mir), die Sache im Akkusativ (das Büro).

items.hr: Ein Kollege gibt mir den Schlüssel für den Eingang.
items.en: A colleague gives me the key for the entrance.
items.note: geben follows the same pattern. der Schlüssel becomes den Schlüssel in the Akkusativ.

items.hr: In der ersten Woche arbeitet mich eine Kollegin ein.
items.en: In the first week a colleague trains me in.
items.note: einarbeiten is separable and takes the person in the Akkusativ: Sie arbeitet mich ein.

items.hr: Können Sie mir bitte sagen, wo die Küche ist?
items.en: Could you please tell me where the kitchen is?
items.note: Polite request with können. After wo the verb moves to the end of the clause.

activities.title: Am ersten Tag
activities.intro: Practise self introductions and the dative and accusative pattern of everyday workplace verbs.

questions.prompt: Welche Form passt? 'Meine Chefin zeigt ____ das Büro.'
questions.explanation: zeigen puts the person in the Dativ, so ich becomes mir. The thing shown, das Büro, is the accusative object.

questions.prompt: How do you introduce yourself politely on your first day?
questions.explanation: A question with dürfen is the polite way in. The last option is an order to the other person, not an introduction.

questions.prompt: Complete the separable verb: 'Ich ____ mich kurz vor: Ich bin neu im Team.'
questions.explanation: vorstellen splits in a main clause: the conjugated part stelle stays in second position, the prefix vor goes to the end.

questions.prompt: Complete with the correct pronoun: 'In der ersten Woche arbeitet ____ eine Kollegin ein.'
questions.explanation: einarbeiten puts the person being trained in the Akkusativ, so ich becomes mich. The separable prefix ein closes the clause.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'My boss shows me the office.'
questions.explanation: Subject, verb, then the dative person before the accusative thing. Pronouns in the Dativ come early in the sentence.

activities.title: Willkommen im Team
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. It is your first day and a colleague shows you around, so you introduce yourself and ask for what you need.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Morgen! Sie sind sicher die neue Kollegin. Herzlich willkommen.
lines.en: Good morning! You must be the new colleague. Welcome.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Morgen. Darf ich mich kurz vorstellen? Ich bin Sara Weber.
lines.en: Good morning. May I briefly introduce myself? I am Sara Weber.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Freut mich. Ich zeige Ihnen zuerst das Büro und dann die Küche.
lines.en: Pleased to meet you. I will show you the office first and then the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gern. Können Sie mir auch erklären, wie das Telefon funktioniert?
lines.en: Gladly. Could you also explain to me how the phone works?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Natürlich. Und hier ist Ihr Schlüssel für den Eingang.
lines.en: Of course. And here is your key for the entrance.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vielen Dank. Wer arbeitet mich in dieser Woche ein?
lines.en: Thank you very much. Who is training me in this week?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Eine Kollegin aus dem Lager. Sie kommt gleich zu Ihnen.
lines.en: A colleague from the warehouse. She is coming to you shortly.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sehr gut. Dann warte ich hier und lese schon die Unterlagen.
lines.en: Very good. Then I will wait here and start reading the documents.

days.title: Mit Kollegen sprechen
days.objective: You can handle the switch between Sie and du at work, make suggestions to colleagues and keep safe small talk going.
days.paretoFocus: Getting the du and Sie question right is the single clearest social signal at a German workplace, and nobody explains the rules to you.
drills: Write the sentence a colleague uses to offer du and two possible answers, one accepting and one declining.
Write three suggestions to a colleague using Sollen wir and Wollen wir.
List three safe small talk topics and two you would avoid at work.

items: Who normally offers the switch to du, and can you decline?
Where does the infinitive go in a Sollen wir question?
What changes about the pronoun once you agree to du?

activities.title: Duzen oder siezen?
activities.intro: With new colleagues you start with Sie. The switch to du is offered, not simply taken, and the offer normally comes from the older colleague or from the person higher up. You may accept or politely stay with Sie, and suggestions to colleagues are usually made with Sollen wir or Wollen wir plus an infinitive at the end.

items.hr: Wollen wir uns duzen? Ich bin die Sabine.
items.en: Shall we use du with each other? I am Sabine.
items.note: duzen = to say du, siezen = to say Sie. Ich bin die Sabine with the article is a warm, informal way to give a first name.

items.hr: Sehr gern. Ich bin der Tom.
items.en: Very gladly. I am Tom.
items.note: The normal way to accept. From that moment you use du and the first name with that one person only.

items.hr: Ich bleibe lieber beim Sie, das ist mir angenehmer.
items.en: I would rather stay with Sie, that is more comfortable for me.
items.note: You may decline. beim Sie bleiben is the fixed expression, and nobody has to switch.

items.hr: Sollen wir zusammen Mittag essen?
items.en: Shall we have lunch together?
items.note: Sollen wir or Wollen wir plus the infinitive at the end. This is the everyday way to suggest something.

items.hr: Wie war Ihr Wochenende? Haben Sie etwas Schönes gemacht?
items.en: How was your weekend? Did you do anything nice?
items.note: Safe topics: das Wochenende, das Wetter, der Urlaub. Better avoided: das Gehalt und die Politik.

activities.title: Im Pausenraum
activities.intro: Practise the du and Sie conventions and the way colleagues suggest things to each other.

questions.prompt: In a German workplace, who normally offers the switch to du?
options: The newest colleague in the team
The older colleague or the person higher up
Nobody, the switch is not allowed
questions.answer: The older colleague or the person higher up
questions.explanation: The offer comes from the older or more senior person. Waiting for it is safer than offering it yourself.

questions.prompt: Welcher Satz ist ein Vorschlag?
questions.explanation: Sollen wir plus infinitive turns a plan into a suggestion. The first option simply states a fact.

questions.prompt: Complete the offer: 'Wollen wir uns ____? Ich bin die Sabine.'
questions.explanation: duzen means to address each other with du. Its opposite is siezen.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite refusal: 'Ich bleibe lieber ____ Sie.'
questions.explanation: The fixed expression is beim Sie bleiben, with bei plus dem contracted to beim.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Shall we have lunch together?'
questions.explanation: In a yes or no question the conjugated verb comes first and the infinitive goes to the very end.

activities.title: In der Pause
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague makes small talk in the break and offers you the switch to du.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wie war Ihr Wochenende? Haben Sie etwas Schönes gemacht?
lines.en: How was your weekend? Did you do anything nice?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, ich war mit meiner Schwester am See. Und Sie?
lines.en: Yes, I was at the lake with my sister. And you?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ich habe nur geschlafen. Übrigens, wollen wir uns duzen? Ich bin die Sabine.
lines.en: I just slept. By the way, shall we use du with each other? I am Sabine.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sehr gern. Ich bin der Tom.
lines.en: Very gladly. I am Tom.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Schön. Sollen wir morgen zusammen Mittag essen?
lines.en: Nice. Shall we have lunch together tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, gern. Sagt unser Chef eigentlich auch du zu allen?
lines.en: Yes, gladly. Does our boss actually say du to everyone as well?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Das entscheidet er selbst. Bleib bei ihm lieber erst mal beim Sie.
lines.en: He decides that himself. With him you should stay with Sie for now.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut, das mache ich. Danke für den Tipp.
lines.en: Good, I will do that. Thanks for the tip.

days.title: Urlaub beantragen
days.objective: You can request specific holiday dates politely and explain your plans using an infinitive clause with zu.
days.paretoFocus: The zu infinitive is what turns a blunt announcement into a request, and holiday is where most learners need it first.
drills: Write three requests beginning with Wäre es möglich and ending with a zu infinitive.
Write two sentences with Ich habe vor about your own plans.
Write one sentence with a separable verb in its zu form, such as mitnehmen or abgeben.

items: Where exactly does zu stand in an infinitive clause?
What happens to zu when the verb is separable?
Which polite opening turns a demand into a request?

activities.title: Urlaub beantragen
activities.intro: German has a very common clause type you have not used yet: an infinitive with zu, hanging off an expression like Es ist möglich, Ich habe vor or Ich hoffe. The zu stands directly in front of the infinitive at the end of the clause, and a comma separates the two parts. Combined with the polite Wäre, this is exactly how holiday is requested.

items.hr: Wäre es möglich, im August zwei Wochen Urlaub zu nehmen?
items.en: Would it be possible to take two weeks of holiday in August?
items.note: The zu infinitive sits at the end: zu nehmen. Wäre makes it a request rather than an announcement.

items.hr: Ich habe vor, den Urlaub mit meiner Familie zu verbringen.
items.en: I am planning to spend the holiday with my family.
items.note: vorhaben = to plan. The main clause comes first, then a comma, then the zu clause.

items.hr: Ich habe vor, meine Kinder mitzunehmen.
items.en: I am planning to take my children with me.
items.note: With separable verbs zu goes inside the word: mitnehmen becomes mitzunehmen, abgeben becomes abzugeben.

items.hr: Bitte geben Sie den Urlaubsantrag rechtzeitig ab.
items.en: Please hand in the holiday request in good time.
items.note: der Urlaubsantrag. abgeben is separable, so the prefix ab goes to the end.

items.hr: Ich hoffe, meinen Resturlaub noch in diesem Jahr zu nehmen.
items.en: I hope to take my remaining holiday still this year.
items.note: der Resturlaub = the days you have left. Many contracts let you carry a few over until the end of March.

activities.title: Der Urlaubsantrag
activities.intro: Practise the infinitive clause with zu and the polite way to ask for time off.

questions.prompt: Welche Form passt? 'Ich habe vor, im Juli nach Spanien ____.'
questions.explanation: In an infinitive clause zu stands directly before the infinitive, and the pair closes the clause.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is the politest way to ask your boss for time off?
options: Wäre es möglich, im August Urlaub zu nehmen?
Geben Sie mir Urlaub im August.
questions.answer: Wäre es möglich, im August Urlaub zu nehmen?
questions.explanation: Wäre es möglich turns the request into a question and leaves your boss room to answer. The others state or demand.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich habe vor, meinen Resturlaub im Dezember ____ nehmen.'
questions.explanation: The infinitive clause needs zu immediately before the infinitive at the end.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Bitte geben Sie Ihren ____ mindestens vier Wochen vorher ab.'
questions.explanation: der Urlaubsantrag, here in the Akkusativ after geben. The compound is masculine because der Antrag is.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Would it be possible to take two weeks of holiday?'
questions.explanation: The question opens with Wäre es möglich, then the infinitive clause follows with zu nehmen at the very end.

activities.title: Beim Chef
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You ask your boss for two weeks off and negotiate the dates when the first choice does not work.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sie wollten mit mir sprechen. Worum geht es?
lines.en: You wanted to speak with me. What is it about?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Wäre es möglich, im August zwei Wochen Urlaub zu nehmen?
lines.en: Would it be possible to take two weeks of holiday in August?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Welche Wochen genau meinen Sie?
lines.en: Which weeks exactly do you mean?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Die erste und die zweite. Ich habe vor, mit meiner Familie zu verreisen.
lines.en: The first and the second. I am planning to travel with my family.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: In der ersten Woche haben schon zwei Kolleginnen frei. Ginge auch die dritte?
lines.en: In the first week two colleagues already have time off. Would the third work too?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, das geht. Dann nehme ich die zweite und die dritte Woche.
lines.en: Yes, that works. Then I will take the second and the third week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gut. Geben Sie mir bitte den Urlaubsantrag bis Freitag ab.
lines.en: Good. Please hand me the holiday request by Friday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mache ich. Vielen Dank für die schnelle Antwort.
lines.en: I will. Thank you very much for the quick answer.

days.title: Schule und Ausbildung in Deutschland
days.objective: You can describe how the German school system is organised and explain what a duale Ausbildung is, using sequencing words.
days.paretoFocus: This system comes up constantly in conversation and has no direct equivalent in most countries, so being able to describe it is genuinely useful.
drills: Write four sentences describing the path from primary school to a first job, using zuerst, danach, dann and schließlich.
Explain a duale Ausbildung in two German sentences.
Write two questions you could ask a parent about their child's school path.

items: How many years does primary school normally last, and what comes next?
Which qualification is the usual route to university?
What are the two places a person in a duale Ausbildung spends the week in?

activities.title: Das Schulsystem und die Ausbildung
activities.intro: The German system splits children into different school types quite early and then offers two main routes after school: university study or vocational training in a company. Sequencing words like zuerst, danach, dann and schließlich hold the description together, and nach takes the Dativ.

items.hr: Zuerst gehen alle Kinder vier Jahre in die Grundschule.
items.en: First all children go to primary school for four years.
items.note: die Grundschule. In einigen Bundesländern dauert sie sechs Jahre, denn Bildung ist Ländersache.

items.hr: Danach besuchen sie verschiedene Schulformen, zum Beispiel die Hauptschule, die Realschule oder das Gymnasium.
items.en: After that they attend different school types, for example the Hauptschule, the Realschule or the Gymnasium.
items.note: Manche Bundesländer fassen mehrere Formen in einer Gesamtschule zusammen. Die Namen sind nicht überall gleich.

items.hr: Am Gymnasium macht man nach der zwölften oder dreizehnten Klasse das Abitur.
items.en: At the Gymnasium you take the Abitur after the twelfth or thirteenth year.
items.note: das Abitur ist der übliche Weg zur Universität. Man kann es auch später nachholen.

items.hr: Viele junge Leute machen eine duale Ausbildung: Sie arbeiten in einem Betrieb und gehen gleichzeitig in die Berufsschule.
items.en: Many young people do a dual vocational training: they work in a company and attend a vocational school at the same time.
items.note: dual = in two places at once. Sie dauert meistens zwei bis dreieinhalb Jahre.

items.hr: Nach der Ausbildung kann man als Fachkraft arbeiten oder später noch studieren.
items.en: After the training you can work as a skilled worker or study later on.
items.note: nach plus Dativ. studieren means at a university, lernen is to study in the general sense.

activities.title: Wege nach der Schule
activities.intro: Practise describing the school system and the two routes that follow it.

questions.prompt: What is a duale Ausbildung?
options: Two university degrees at the same time
Paid training in a company combined with vocational school
An evening school for adults only
A second attempt at the Abitur
questions.answer: Paid training in a company combined with vocational school
questions.explanation: The trainee is employed and paid by a company and spends one or two days a week at a vocational school.

questions.prompt: Welche Präposition passt? '____ der Grundschule besuchen die Kinder eine weiterführende Schule.'
questions.explanation: nach plus Dativ marks what comes afterwards in a sequence, here der Grundschule.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Am Gymnasium macht man am Ende das ____.'
questions.explanation: das Abitur is the school leaving qualification that opens the way to university study.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Die Auszubildenden arbeiten im Betrieb und gehen an ein oder zwei Tagen pro Woche in die ____.'
questions.explanation: die Berufsschule is the school half of the dual system, where the theory is taught.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'First all children go to primary school for four years.'
questions.explanation: Zuerst opens the sentence, so the verb stays in second position and the subject alle Kinder comes after it.

activities.title: Wie geht es nach der Schule weiter?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A parent new to the country asks you how the school system works and you explain the routes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Meine Tochter ist zehn. Bald wechselt sie die Schule. Wie läuft das hier?
lines.en: My daughter is ten. Soon she will change schools. How does that work here?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nach der Grundschule gibt es verschiedene Schulformen, zum Beispiel die Realschule oder das Gymnasium.
lines.en: After primary school there are different school types, for example the Realschule or the Gymnasium.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wenn sie später studieren möchte?
lines.en: And if she wants to study at university later?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dann braucht sie das Abitur. Das macht man meistens am Gymnasium.
lines.en: Then she needs the Abitur. You usually take it at the Gymnasium.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Und wenn sie lieber praktisch arbeiten will?
lines.en: And if she would rather work practically?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dann passt eine duale Ausbildung. Sie arbeitet in einem Betrieb und geht gleichzeitig in die Berufsschule.
lines.en: Then a dual vocational training fits. She works in a company and attends a vocational school at the same time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Verdient sie dabei schon Geld?
lines.en: Does she already earn money doing that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, Auszubildende bekommen jeden Monat eine Vergütung. Danach kann sie sofort als Fachkraft arbeiten.
lines.en: Yes, trainees receive a monthly payment. After that she can work as a skilled worker straight away.

days.title: Eine Weiterbildung finden
days.objective: You can ask about a training course, describe your goal with um ... zu and ask who pays for it.
days.paretoFocus: Saying why you are doing something is one of the most reused structures in German, and a course enquiry is the natural place to learn it.
drills: Write three sentences with um ... zu giving your own reasons for learning something.
Write two questions about a course: when it runs and what it costs.
Write one sentence with a separable verb in the um ... zu construction.

items: What condition must be met for um ... zu to be usable?
Where does zu go with a separable verb such as teilnehmen?
What does berufsbegleitend tell you about a course?

activities.title: Eine Weiterbildung machen
activities.intro: To say what something is for, German uses um plus a zu infinitive at the end. It only works when both halves of the sentence share the same subject. This is the natural language for asking about courses, and it builds directly on the plain zu infinitive you already know.

items.hr: Ich mache eine Weiterbildung, um mich beruflich zu verbessern.
items.en: I am doing further training in order to improve professionally.
items.note: um ... zu plus Infinitiv gives the purpose. Both parts must share one subject, here ich.

items.hr: Der Kurs findet berufsbegleitend am Abend statt.
items.en: The course runs alongside your job in the evening.
items.note: berufsbegleitend = while you keep working. stattfinden ist trennbar: Der Kurs findet statt.

items.hr: Um an dem Kurs teilzunehmen, brauche ich keine Vorkenntnisse.
items.en: In order to take part in the course, I need no prior knowledge.
items.note: With separable verbs zu goes inside: teilnehmen becomes teilzunehmen. teilnehmen an plus Dativ.

items.hr: Am Ende bekomme ich ein Zertifikat über die Teilnahme.
items.en: At the end I receive a certificate of participation.
items.note: das Zertifikat, die Teilnahme. Ask about this early, since not every course gives one.

items.hr: Ich frage im Betrieb nach, ob die Firma die Kosten übernimmt.
items.en: I will ask at work whether the company covers the costs.
items.note: nachfragen ist trennbar. die Kosten übernehmen = to pay for something. Manchmal gibt es auch eine Förderung.

activities.title: Kurs und Ziel
activities.intro: Practise expressing purpose with um ... zu and the vocabulary of training courses.

questions.prompt: Welches Wort passt? 'Ich lerne abends, ____ eine bessere Stelle zu finden.'
questions.explanation: The clause already ends in zu finden, so the purpose marker at the front must be um. Both halves share the subject ich.

questions.prompt: 'Der Kurs ist berufsbegleitend.' What does that mean?
options: You have to give up your job for it
The course runs alongside your job
The course is only for beginners
questions.answer: The course runs alongside your job
questions.explanation: berufsbegleitend courses are timetabled in the evening or at weekends so that you can keep working.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Am Ende des Kurses bekomme ich ein ____ über die Teilnahme.'
questions.explanation: das Zertifikat is the written proof that you completed the course.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ich frage im Betrieb nach, ob die Firma die ____ übernimmt.'
questions.explanation: die Kosten is used in the plural. die Kosten übernehmen means to cover the expense.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'I am doing further training in order to improve professionally.'
questions.explanation: The main clause comes first, then um opens the purpose clause and zu verbessern closes it.

activities.title: Beratung zur Weiterbildung
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You enquire about an evening course and explain what you want to achieve with it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sie möchten sich weiterbilden. Was ist Ihr Ziel?
lines.en: You would like to do further training. What is your goal?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ich mache eine Weiterbildung, um später im Büro zu arbeiten.
lines.en: I am doing further training in order to work in an office later.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wir haben einen Abendkurs. Er findet zweimal pro Woche statt.
lines.en: We have an evening course. It takes place twice a week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Also berufsbegleitend? Das passt gut, ich arbeite tagsüber.
lines.en: So alongside my job? That fits well, I work during the day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Genau. Um an dem Kurs teilzunehmen, brauchen Sie keine Vorkenntnisse.
lines.en: Exactly. In order to take part in the course you need no prior knowledge.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Und was kostet er?
lines.en: And what does it cost?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Sechshundert Euro. Fragen Sie in Ihrem Betrieb nach, oft übernimmt die Firma die Kosten.
lines.en: Six hundred euros. Ask at your company, often the employer covers the costs.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das mache ich. Am Ende bekomme ich ein Zertifikat, richtig?
lines.en: I will do that. At the end I receive a certificate, correct?

days.title: Fahrkarten kaufen und Verbindungen suchen
days.objective: Buy a train ticket at a counter, choose between a one way and a return, and ask the three questions that decide whether the ticket actually works for your journey.
days.paretoFocus: Almost every trip in a German speaking country starts with the same four decisions, one way or return, class, discount, and whether the ticket is tied to one train, so this handful of phrases covers the whole transaction.
drills: Say out loud three versions of the same ticket request: one way, return, and second class.
Describe one journey with a single change: where you get on, where you change, and where you get off.
Ask three counter questions from memory, one about a discount, one about changing trains, and one about which train the ticket is valid for.

items: How do you ask for a one way ticket, and how for a return?
Where does the prefix of umsteigen go in a main clause?
What is the difference between a ticket tied to one train and a full price ticket?

activities.title: Die Fahrkarte kaufen
activities.intro: At a ticket counter you are expected to say the destination first and the type of ticket second, which is why einfach and hin und zurück come at the end of the request. Two verbs carry most of the conversation, umsteigen for changing trains and gelten for what a ticket is valid for. Note that every ticket type is a noun and needs its article.

items.hr: Einmal nach Hamburg, bitte, einfach.
items.en: One ticket to Hamburg, please, one way.
items.note: einfach = one way, hin und zurück = return

items.hr: Ich hätte gern eine Fahrkarte hin und zurück, zweite Klasse.
items.en: I would like a return ticket, second class.
items.note: Ich hätte gern is the normal polite form at a counter

items.hr: Muss ich unterwegs umsteigen, oder fährt der Zug durch?
items.en: Do I have to change on the way, or does the train go straight through?
items.note: umsteigen is separable: ich steige in Köln um

items.hr: Gibt es eine Ermäßigung für Studenten?
items.en: Is there a discount for students?
items.note: die Ermäßigung, the reduced fare

items.hr: Die günstige Fahrkarte gilt nur für diesen einen Zug, die zum Normalpreis gilt den ganzen Tag.
items.en: The cheap ticket is valid only for this one train, the full price one is valid all day.
items.note: gelten für, to be valid for

activities.title: Am Fahrkartenschalter üben
activities.intro: Practise asking for tickets, discounts and connections.

questions.prompt: You want a return ticket. What do you ask for?
questions.explanation: hin und zurück literally means there and back, so it is the return ticket. einfach is the one way ticket.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the separable verb umsteigen correctly in a main clause?
options: Ich bin um in Köln gestiegen.
Ich steige um in Köln ein.
questions.explanation: In a main clause the separable prefix um goes to the very end, so the conjugated stem steige stays in second position.

questions.prompt: Ask about a reduced fare: Gibt es eine ___ für Studenten?
questions.explanation: die Ermäßigung is the noun for a price reduction, and like every German noun it is capitalised. The ss sound after the long ä is written ß.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb meaning to be valid: Die günstige Fahrkarte ___ nur für diesen einen Zug.
questions.explanation: gelten is a strong verb with a vowel change in the third person singular, so es gilt, not es geltet.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Do I have to change trains in Frankfurt?'
questions.explanation: In a yes or no question the modal verb comes first, and the infinitive umsteigen goes to the end of the clause.

activities.title: Eine Fahrkarte nach Leipzig
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are buying a ticket at a counter and need to settle the type of ticket, the change of trains and the discount.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Guten Tag, was kann ich für Sie tun?
lines.en: Hello, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guten Tag. Ich hätte gern eine Fahrkarte nach Leipzig, hin und zurück.
lines.en: Hello. I would like a ticket to Leipzig, return.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Für heute? Der nächste Zug fährt um 14:35.
lines.en: For today? The next train leaves at 14:35.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ja, für heute. Muss ich unterwegs umsteigen?
lines.en: Yes, for today. Do I have to change on the way?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Einmal in Erfurt, der Anschluss fährt vom selben Gleis ab.
lines.en: Once in Erfurt, the connection leaves from the same platform.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut. Gibt es eine Ermäßigung, wenn ich erst nach neun Uhr fahre?
lines.en: Good. Is there a discount if I only travel after nine?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja, dann wird es billiger, aber die Fahrkarte gilt nur für diesen einen Zug.
lines.en: Yes, then it gets cheaper, but the ticket is valid only for this one train.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Das ist in Ordnung, ich nehme sie. Muss ich sie vorher noch entwerten?
lines.en: That is fine, I will take it. Do I still have to validate it beforehand?

days.title: Am Bahnhof, Verspätung und Anschluss
days.objective: Understand announcements about delays and cancellations and ask the two questions that actually matter, whether you will still reach your connection and what compensation you are entitled to.
days.paretoFocus: Things go wrong on the rails often enough that the vocabulary of disruption is used more than the vocabulary of a smooth journey, and knowing the two key questions turns you from a passive passenger into someone who can act.
drills: Announce three delays out loud with different numbers of minutes, using the verb that belongs with Verspätung.
Say what happens when a train is cancelled, naming the cancellation and the replacement bus service.
Practise the two questions that matter in a disruption until you can ask both without pausing.

items: Which verb goes with Verspätung, haben or sein?
What time of day is halb acht?
Which question checks if your connecting train is still reachable?

activities.title: Verspätung, Ausfall und Anschluss
activities.intro: German delay language is built on a few fixed combinations that you should learn whole. Verspätung goes with haben, never with sein, and a cancelled train uses the separable verb ausfallen. Announcements also love long compound nouns, so learn der Schienenersatzverkehr as one block.

items.hr: Der Zug hat zwanzig Minuten Verspätung.
items.en: The train is twenty minutes late.
items.note: Verspätung haben, never Verspätung sein

items.hr: Der Zug um halb acht fällt heute aus.
items.en: The 7:30 train is cancelled today.
items.note: halb acht means 7:30, halfway to eight, and der Ausfall is the cancellation

items.hr: Erreiche ich meinen Anschluss in Hannover noch?
items.en: Will I still reach my connection in Hannover?
items.note: the first question that matters in a disruption

items.hr: Zwischen Kassel und Göttingen fährt heute ein Schienenersatzverkehr.
items.en: Between Kassel and Göttingen there is a replacement bus service today.
items.note: der Schienenersatzverkehr, buses running instead of trains

items.hr: Ab wie viel Verspätung bekomme ich eine Entschädigung?
items.en: From how much delay onwards do I get compensation?
items.note: die Entschädigung, the second question that matters

activities.title: Störungen verstehen
activities.intro: Practise the fixed combinations used for delays and cancellations.

questions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says that the train is twenty minutes late?
options: Der Zug ist zwanzig Minuten Verspätung.
Der Zug hat zwanzig Minuten Verspätung.
Der Zug macht zwanzig Minuten Verspätung.
Der Zug gibt zwanzig Minuten Verspätung.
questions.answer: Der Zug hat zwanzig Minuten Verspätung.
questions.explanation: Verspätung is a noun that is possessed, so German uses haben. A train that ist verspätet is possible too, but then no noun follows.

questions.prompt: In German time expressions, halb acht means:
questions.explanation: German counts towards the coming hour, so halb acht is half way to eight, that is 7:30. It never means 8:30.

questions.prompt: Complete the separable verb: Der Zug nach Bremen ___ heute aus.
questions.explanation: ausfallen is separable, so the stem fällt takes second position with the vowel change of the third person singular, and the prefix aus stays at the end.

questions.prompt: Ask about money after a long delay: Ab wie viel Verspätung bekomme ich eine ___?
questions.explanation: die Entschädigung is the payment you receive for a loss, and it is capitalised like every noun.

questions.prompt: Build the German for: 'Will I still reach my connection?'
questions.explanation: The verb opens the yes or no question, der Anschluss is the accusative object so it becomes meinen Anschluss, and noch closes the sentence.

activities.title: Auf dem Bahnsteig
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your train is delayed and you have to find out about the connection and the compensation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Achtung, der Zug nach Hannover fährt heute von Gleis 9 ab, nicht von Gleis 4.
lines.en: Attention, the train to Hannover leaves from platform 9 today, not from platform 4.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Entschuldigung, hat der Zug auch Verspätung?
lines.en: Excuse me, is the train also delayed?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ja, etwa fünfundzwanzig Minuten, wegen einer Störung auf der Strecke.
lines.en: Yes, about twenty five minutes, because of a problem on the line.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Erreiche ich dann meinen Anschluss um halb acht noch?
lines.en: Will I still reach my connection at 7:30 then?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Wahrscheinlich nicht, der letzte Anschluss wartet nur zehn Minuten.
lines.en: Probably not, the last connection waits only ten minutes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Und wenn ich ihn verpasse, ab wann bekomme ich eine Entschädigung?
lines.en: And if I miss it, from when do I get compensation?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ab einer Stunde können Sie einen Teil des Fahrpreises zurückverlangen.
lines.en: From one hour onwards you can claim part of the fare back.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Gut, dann fülle ich das Formular gleich im Zug aus.
lines.en: Good, then I will fill in the form on the train straight away.

days.title: Am Flughafen
days.objective: Check a bag in, talk your way through security and understand a change of gate, using the airport nouns and the separable verbs that go with them.
days.paretoFocus: The airport sequence is the same everywhere and always in the same order, so a small set of nouns and four separable verbs let you handle the whole journey from the counter to boarding.
drills: Pack an imaginary bag out loud, naming three things for the hold and three for the hand luggage.
Say the whole airport sequence in order, from the counter to boarding, in five sentences.
Ask two questions, one about the departure time and one about the gate.

items: Which verb means to check a bag into the hold?
What are the German nouns for the departure and the arrival of a plane?
What do you take out of your bag at the security check?

activities.title: Der Weg durch den Flughafen
activities.intro: Airport German runs on separable verbs, aufgeben for handing a bag over, herausnehmen for taking something out and abfliegen for departing.
